478:     blob: string;
479:     mimeType?: string | null;
480:     uri: string;
481: };
482: /**
483:  * Schema for boolean properties in an elicitation form.
484:  */
485: export type BooleanPropertySchema = {
486:     /**
487:      * Default value.
488:      */
489:     default?: boolean | null;
490:     /**
491:      * Human-readable description.
492:      */
493:     description?: string | null;
494:     /**
495:      * Optional title for the property.
496:      */
497:     title?: string | null;
498: };
499: /**
500:  * Notification to cancel ongoing operations for a session.
501:  *
502:  * See protocol docs: [Cancellation](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#cancellation)
503:  */
504: export type CancelNotification = {
505:     /**
506:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
507:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
508:      * these keys.
509:      *
510:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
511:      */
512:     _meta?: {
513:         [key: string]: unknown;
514:     } | null;
515:     /**
516:      * The ID of the session to cancel operations for.
517:      */
518:     sessionId: SessionId;
519: };
520: /**
521:  * **UNSTABLE**
522:  *
523:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
524:  *
525:  * Notification to cancel an ongoing request.
526:  *
527:  * See protocol docs: [Cancellation](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/cancellation)
528:  *
529:  * @experimental
530:  */
531: export type CancelRequestNotification = {
532:     /**
533:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
534:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
535:      * these keys.
536:      *
537:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
538:      */
539:     _meta?: {
540:         [key: string]: unknown;
541:     } | null;
542:     /**
543:      * The ID of the request to cancel.
544:      */
545:     requestId: RequestId;
546: };
547: /**
548:  * Capabilities supported by the client.
549:  *
550:  * Advertised during initialization to inform the agent about
551:  * available features and methods.
552:  *
553:  * See protocol docs: [Client Capabilities](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization#client-capabilities)
554:  */
555: export type ClientCapabilities = {
556:     /**
557:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
558:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
559:      * these keys.
560:      *
561:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
562:      */
563:     _meta?: {
564:         [key: string]: unknown;
565:     } | null;
566:     /**
567:      * **UNSTABLE**
568:      *
569:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
570:      *
571:      * Authentication capabilities supported by the client.
572:      * Determines which authentication method types the agent may include
573:      * in its `InitializeResponse`.
574:      *
575:      * @experimental
576:      */
577:     auth?: AuthCapabilities;
578:     /**
579:      * **UNSTABLE**
580:      *
581:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
582:      *
583:      * Elicitation capabilities supported by the client.
584:      * Determines which elicitation modes the agent may use.
585:      *
586:      * @experimental
587:      */
588:     elicitation?: ElicitationCapabilities | null;
589:     /**
590:      * File system capabilities supported by the client.
591:      * Determines which file operations the agent can request.
592:      */
593:     fs?: FileSystemCapabilities;
594:     /**
595:      * **UNSTABLE**
596:      *
597:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
598:      *
599:      * NES (Next Edit Suggestions) capabilities supported by the client.
600:      *
601:      * @experimental
602:      */
603:     nes?: ClientNesCapabilities | null;
604:     /**
605:      * **UNSTABLE**
606:      *
607:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
608:      *
609:      * The position encodings supported by the client, in order of preference.
610:      *
611:      * @experimental
612:      */
613:     positionEncodings?: Array<PositionEncodingKind>;
614:     /**
615:      * Whether the Client support all `terminal*` methods.
616:      */
617:     terminal?: boolean;
618: };
619: /**
620:  * NES capabilities advertised by the client during initialization.
621:  */
622: export type ClientNesCapabilities = {
623:     /**
624:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
625:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
626:      * these keys.
627:      *
628:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
629:      */
630:     _meta?: {
631:         [key: string]: unknown;
632:     } | null;
633:     /**
634:      * Whether the client supports the `jump` suggestion kind.
635:      */
636:     jump?: NesJumpCapabilities | null;
637:     /**
638:      * Whether the client supports the `rename` suggestion kind.
639:      */
640:     rename?: NesRenameCapabilities | null;
641:     /**
642:      * Whether the client supports the `searchAndReplace` suggestion kind.
643:      */
644:     searchAndReplace?: NesSearchAndReplaceCapabilities | null;
645: };
646: export type ClientNotification = {
647:     method: string;
648:     params?: CancelNotification | DidOpenDocumentNotification | DidChangeDocumentNotification | DidCloseDocumentNotification | DidSaveDocumentNotification | DidFocusDocumentNotification | AcceptNesNotification | RejectNesNotification | ExtNotification | null;
649: };
650: export type ClientRequest = {
651:     id: RequestId;
652:     method: string;
653:     params?: InitializeRequest | AuthenticateRequest | LogoutRequest | NewSessionRequest | LoadSessionRequest | ListSessionsRequest | ForkSessionRequest | ResumeSessionRequest | CloseSessionRequest | SetSessionModeRequest | SetSessionConfigOptionRequest | PromptRequest | SetSessionModelRequest | StartNesRequest | SuggestNesRequest | CloseNesRequest | ExtRequest | null;
654: };
655: export type ClientResponse = {
656:     id: RequestId;
657:     /**
658:      * All possible responses that a client can send to an agent.
659:      *
660:      * This enum is used internally for routing RPC responses. You typically won't need
661:      * to use this directly - the responses are handled automatically by the connection.
662:      *
663:      * These are responses to the corresponding `AgentRequest` variants.
664:      */
665:     result: WriteTextFileResponse | ReadTextFileResponse | RequestPermissionResponse | CreateTerminalResponse | TerminalOutputResponse | ReleaseTerminalResponse | WaitForTerminalExitResponse | KillTerminalResponse | ElicitationResponse | ExtResponse;
666: } | {
667:     error: Error;
668:     id: RequestId;
669: };
670: /**
671:  * Request to close an NES session.
672:  *
673:  * The agent **must** cancel any ongoing work related to the NES session
674:  * and then free up any resources associated with the session.
675:  */
676: export type CloseNesRequest = {
677:     /**
678:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
679:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
680:      * these keys.
681:      *
682:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
683:      */
684:     _meta?: {
685:         [key: string]: unknown;
686:     } | null;
687:     /**
688:      * The ID of the NES session to close.
689:      */
690:     sessionId: SessionId;
691: };
692: /**
693:  * Response from closing an NES session.
694:  */
695: export type CloseNesResponse = {
696:     /**
697:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
698:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
699:      * these keys.
700:      *
701:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
702:      */
703:     _meta?: {
704:         [key: string]: unknown;
705:     } | null;
706: };
707: /**
708:  * **UNSTABLE**
709:  *
710:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
711:  *
712:  * Request parameters for closing an active session.
713:  *
714:  * If supported, the agent **must** cancel any ongoing work related to the session
715:  * (treat it as if `session/cancel` was called) and then free up any resources
716:  * associated with the session.
717:  *
718:  * Only available if the Agent supports the `session.close` capability.
719:  *
720:  * @experimental
721:  */
722: export type CloseSessionRequest = {
723:     /**
724:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
725:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
726:      * these keys.
727:      *
728:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
729:      */
730:     _meta?: {
731:         [key: string]: unknown;
732:     } | null;
733:     /**
734:      * The ID of the session to close.
735:      */
736:     sessionId: SessionId;
737: };
738: /**
739:  * **UNSTABLE**
740:  *
741:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
742:  *
743:  * Response from closing a session.
744:  *
745:  * @experimental
746:  */
747: export type CloseSessionResponse = {
748:     /**
749:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
750:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
751:      * these keys.
752:      *
753:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
754:      */
755:     _meta?: {
756:         [key: string]: unknown;
757:     } | null;
758: };
759: /**
760:  * Session configuration options have been updated.
761:  */
762: export type ConfigOptionUpdate = {
763:     /**
764:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
765:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
766:      * these keys.
767:      *
768:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
769:      */
770:     _meta?: {
771:         [key: string]: unknown;
772:     } | null;
773:     /**
774:      * The full set of configuration options and their current values.
775:      */
776:     configOptions: Array<SessionConfigOption>;
777: };
778: /**
779:  * Standard content block (text, images, resources).
780:  */
781: export type Content = {
782:     /**
783:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
784:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
785:      * these keys.
786:      *
787:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
788:      */
789:     _meta?: {
790:         [key: string]: unknown;
791:     } | null;
792:     /**
793:      * The actual content block.
794:      */
795:     content: ContentBlock;
796: };
797: /**
798:  * Content blocks represent displayable information in the Agent Client Protocol.
799:  *
800:  * They provide a structured way to handle various types of user-facing content—whether
801:  * it's text from language models, images for analysis, or embedded resources for context.
802:  *
803:  * Content blocks appear in:
804:  * - User prompts sent via `session/prompt`
805:  * - Language model output streamed through `session/update` notifications
806:  * - Progress updates and results from tool calls
807:  *
808:  * This structure is compatible with the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling
809:  * agents to seamlessly forward content from MCP tool outputs without transformation.
810:  *
811:  * See protocol docs: [Content](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/content)
812:  */
813: export type ContentBlock = (TextContent & {
814:     type: "text";
815: }) | (ImageContent & {
816:     type: "image";
817: }) | (AudioContent & {
818:     type: "audio";
819: }) | (ResourceLink & {
820:     type: "resource_link";
821: }) | (EmbeddedResource & {
822:     type: "resource";
823: });
824: /**
825:  * A streamed item of content
826:  */
827: export type ContentChunk = {
828:     /**
829:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
830:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
831:      * these keys.
832:      *
833:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
834:      */
835:     _meta?: {
836:         [key: string]: unknown;
837:     } | null;
838:     /**
839:      * A single item of content
840:      */
841:     content: ContentBlock;
842:     /**
843:      * **UNSTABLE**
844:      *
845:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
846:      *
847:      * A unique identifier for the message this chunk belongs to.
848:      *
849:      * All chunks belonging to the same message share the same `messageId`.
850:      * A change in `messageId` indicates a new message has started.
851:      * Both clients and agents MUST use UUID format for message IDs.
852:      *
853:      * @experimental
854:      */
855:     messageId?: string | null;
856: };
857: /**
858:  * **UNSTABLE**
859:  *
860:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
861:  *
862:  * Cost information for a session.
863:  *
864:  * @experimental
865:  */
866: export type Cost = {
867:     /**
868:      * Total cumulative cost for session.
869:      */
870:     amount: number;
871:     /**
872:      * ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., "USD", "EUR").
873:      */
874:     currency: string;
875: };
876: /**
877:  * Request to create a new terminal and execute a command.
878:  */
879: export type CreateTerminalRequest = {
880:     /**
881:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
882:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
883:      * these keys.
884:      *
885:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
886:      */
887:     _meta?: {
888:         [key: string]: unknown;
889:     } | null;
890:     /**
891:      * Array of command arguments.
892:      */
893:     args?: Array<string>;
894:     /**
895:      * The command to execute.
896:      */
897:     command: string;
898:     /**
899:      * Working directory for the command (absolute path).
900:      */
901:     cwd?: string | null;
902:     /**
903:      * Environment variables for the command.
904:      */
905:     env?: Array<EnvVariable>;
906:     /**
907:      * Maximum number of output bytes to retain.
908:      *
909:      * When the limit is exceeded, the Client truncates from the beginning of the output
910:      * to stay within the limit.
911:      *
912:      * The Client MUST ensure truncation happens at a character boundary to maintain valid
913:      * string output, even if this means the retained output is slightly less than the
914:      * specified limit.
915:      */
916:     outputByteLimit?: number | null;
917:     /**
918:      * The session ID for this request.
919:      */
920:     sessionId: SessionId;
921: };
922: /**
923:  * Response containing the ID of the created terminal.
924:  */
925: export type CreateTerminalResponse = {
926:     /**
927:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
928:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
929:      * these keys.
930:      *
931:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
932:      */
933:     _meta?: {
934:         [key: string]: unknown;
935:     } | null;
936:     /**
937:      * The unique identifier for the created terminal.
938:      */
939:     terminalId: string;
940: };
941: /**
942:  * The current mode of the session has changed
943:  *
944:  * See protocol docs: [Session Modes](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-modes)
945:  */
946: export type CurrentModeUpdate = {
947:     /**
948:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
949:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
950:      * these keys.
951:      *
952:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
953:      */
954:     _meta?: {
955:         [key: string]: unknown;
956:     } | null;
957:     /**
958:      * The ID of the current mode
959:      */
960:     currentModeId: SessionModeId;
961: };
962: /**
963:  * Notification sent when a file is edited.
964:  */
965: export type DidChangeDocumentNotification = {
966:     /**
967:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
968:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
969:      * these keys.
970:      *
971:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
972:      */
973:     _meta?: {
974:         [key: string]: unknown;
975:     } | null;
976:     /**
977:      * The content changes.
978:      */
979:     contentChanges: Array<TextDocumentContentChangeEvent>;
980:     /**
981:      * The session ID for this notification.
982:      */
983:     sessionId: SessionId;
984:     /**
985:      * The URI of the changed document.
986:      */
987:     uri: string;
988:     /**
989:      * The new version number of the document.
990:      */
991:     version: number;
992: };
993: /**
994:  * Notification sent when a file is closed.
995:  */
996: export type DidCloseDocumentNotification = {
997:     /**
998:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
999:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
1000:      * these keys.
1001:      *
1002:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
1003:      */
1004:     _meta?: {
1005:         [key: string]: unknown;
1006:     } | null;
1007:     /**
1008:      * The session ID for this notification.
1009:      */
1010:     sessionId: SessionId;
1011:     /**
1012:      * The URI of the closed document.
1013:      */
1014:     uri: string;
1015: };
1016: /**
1017:  * Notification sent when a file becomes the active editor tab.
1018:  */
1019: export type DidFocusDocumentNotification = {
1020:     /**
1021:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
1022:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
1023:      * these keys.
1024:      *
1025:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
1026:      */
1027:     _meta?: {
1028:         [key: string]: unknown;
1029:     } | null;
1030:     /**
1031:      * The current cursor position.
1032:      */
1033:     position: Position;
1034:     /**
1035:      * The session ID for this notification.
1036:      */
1037:     sessionId: SessionId;
1038:     /**
1039:      * The URI of the focused document.
1040:      */
1041:     uri: string;
1042:     /**
1043:      * The version number of the document.
1044:      */
1045:     version: number;
1046:     /**
1047:      * The portion of the file currently visible in the editor viewport.
1048:      */
1049:     visibleRange: Range;
1050: };
1051: /**
1052:  * Notification sent when a file is opened in the editor.
1053:  */
1054: export type DidOpenDocumentNotification = {
1055:     /**
1056:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
1057:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
1058:      * these keys.
1059:      *
1060:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
1061:      */
1062:     _meta?: {
1063:         [key: string]: unknown;
1064:     } | null;
1065:     /**
1066:      * The language identifier of the document (e.g., "rust", "python").
1067:      */
1068:     languageId: string;
1069:     /**
1070:      * The session ID for this notification.
1071:      */
1072:     sessionId: SessionId;
1073:     /**
1074:      * The full text content of the document.
1075:      */
1076:     text: string;
1077:     /**
1078:      * The URI of the opened document.
1079:      */
1080:     uri: string;
1081:     /**
1082:      * The version number of the document.
1083:      */
1084:     version: number;
1085: };
1086: /**
1087:  * Notification sent when a file is saved.
1088:  */
1089: export type DidSaveDocumentNotification = {
1090:     /**
1091:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
1092:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
1093:      * these keys.
1094:      *
1095:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
1096:      */
1097:     _meta?: {
1098:         [key: string]: unknown;
1099:     } | null;
1100:     /**
1101:      * The session ID for this notification.
1102:      */
1103:     sessionId: SessionId;
1104:     /**
1105:      * The URI of the saved document.
1106:      */
1107:     uri: string;
1108: };
1109: /**
1110:  * A diff representing file modifications.
1111:  *
1112:  * Shows changes to files in a format suitable for display in the client UI.
1113:  *
1114:  * See protocol docs: [Content](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#content)
1115:  */
1116: export type Diff = {
1117:     /**
1118:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
1119:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
1120:      * these keys.
1121:      *
1122:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
1123:      */
1124:     _meta?: {
1125:         [key: string]: unknown;
1126:     } | null;
1127:     /**
1128:      * The new content after modification.
1129:      */
1130:     newText: string;
1131:     /**
1132:      * The original content (None for new files).
1133:      */
1134:     oldText?: string | null;
1135:     /**
1136:      * The file path being modified.
1137:      */
1138:     path: string;
1139: };
1140: /**
1141:  * **UNSTABLE**
1142:  *
1143:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
1144:  *
1145:  * The user accepted the elicitation and provided content.
1146:  *
1147:  * @experimental
1148:  */
1149: export type ElicitationAcceptAction = {
1150:     /**
1151:      * The user-provided content, if any, as an object matching the requested schema.
1152:      */
1153:     content?: {
1154:         [key: string]: ElicitationContentValue;
1155:     } | null;
1156: };
1157: /**
1158:  * **UNSTABLE**
1159:  *
1160:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
1161:  *
1162:  * The user's action in response to an elicitation.
1163:  *
1164:  * @experimental
1165:  */
1166: export type ElicitationAction = (ElicitationAcceptAction & {
1167:     action: "accept";
1168: }) | {
1169:     action: "decline";
1170: } | {
1171:     action: "cancel";
1172: };
1173: /**
1174:  * **UNSTABLE**
1175:  *
1176:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
1177:  *
1178:  * Elicitation capabilities supported by the client.
1179:  *
1180:  * @experimental
1181:  */
1182: export type ElicitationCapabilities = {
1183:     /**
1184:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
1185:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at

159:                   "allOf": [
160:                     {
161:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/ExtNotification"
162:                     }
163:                   ],
164:                   "description": "Handles extension notifications from the agent.\n\nAllows the Agent to send an arbitrary notification that is not part of the ACP spec.\nExtension notifications provide a way to send one-way messages for custom functionality\nwhile maintaining protocol compatibility.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
165:                   "title": "ExtNotification"
166:                 }
167:               ],
168:               "description": "All possible notifications that an agent can send to a client.\n\nThis enum is used internally for routing RPC notifications. You typically won't need\nto use this directly - use the notification methods on the [`Client`] trait instead.\n\nNotifications do not expect a response."
169:             },
170:             {
171:               "type": "null"
172:             }
173:           ]
174:         }
175:       },
176:       "required": ["method"],
177:       "type": "object",
178:       "x-docs-ignore": true
179:     },
180:     "AgentRequest": {
181:       "properties": {
182:         "id": {
183:           "$ref": "#/$defs/RequestId"
184:         },
185:         "method": {
186:           "type": "string"
187:         },
188:         "params": {
189:           "anyOf": [
190:             {
191:               "anyOf": [
192:                 {
193:                   "allOf": [
194:                     {
195:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/WriteTextFileRequest"
196:                     }
197:                   ],
198:                   "description": "Writes content to a text file in the client's file system.\n\nOnly available if the client advertises the `fs.writeTextFile` capability.\nAllows the agent to create or modify files within the client's environment.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Client](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/overview#client)",
199:                   "title": "WriteTextFileRequest"
200:                 },
201:                 {
202:                   "allOf": [
203:                     {
204:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/ReadTextFileRequest"
205:                     }
206:                   ],
207:                   "description": "Reads content from a text file in the client's file system.\n\nOnly available if the client advertises the `fs.readTextFile` capability.\nAllows the agent to access file contents within the client's environment.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Client](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/overview#client)",
208:                   "title": "ReadTextFileRequest"
209:                 },
210:                 {
211:                   "allOf": [
212:                     {
213:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/RequestPermissionRequest"
214:                     }
215:                   ],
216:                   "description": "Requests permission from the user for a tool call operation.\n\nCalled by the agent when it needs user authorization before executing\na potentially sensitive operation. The client should present the options\nto the user and return their decision.\n\nIf the client cancels the prompt turn via `session/cancel`, it MUST\nrespond to this request with `RequestPermissionOutcome::Cancelled`.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Requesting Permission](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#requesting-permission)",
217:                   "title": "RequestPermissionRequest"
218:                 },
219:                 {
220:                   "allOf": [
221:                     {
222:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/CreateTerminalRequest"
223:                     }
224:                   ],
225:                   "description": "Executes a command in a new terminal\n\nOnly available if the `terminal` Client capability is set to `true`.\n\nReturns a `TerminalId` that can be used with other terminal methods\nto get the current output, wait for exit, and kill the command.\n\nThe `TerminalId` can also be used to embed the terminal in a tool call\nby using the `ToolCallContent::Terminal` variant.\n\nThe Agent is responsible for releasing the terminal by using the `terminal/release`\nmethod.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Terminals](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals)",
226:                   "title": "CreateTerminalRequest"
227:                 },
228:                 {
229:                   "allOf": [
230:                     {
231:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/TerminalOutputRequest"
232:                     }
233:                   ],
234:                   "description": "Gets the terminal output and exit status\n\nReturns the current content in the terminal without waiting for the command to exit.\nIf the command has already exited, the exit status is included.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Terminals](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals)",
235:                   "title": "TerminalOutputRequest"
236:                 },
237:                 {
238:                   "allOf": [
239:                     {
240:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/ReleaseTerminalRequest"
241:                     }
242:                   ],
243:                   "description": "Releases a terminal\n\nThe command is killed if it hasn't exited yet. Use `terminal/wait_for_exit`\nto wait for the command to exit before releasing the terminal.\n\nAfter release, the `TerminalId` can no longer be used with other `terminal/*` methods,\nbut tool calls that already contain it, continue to display its output.\n\nThe `terminal/kill` method can be used to terminate the command without releasing\nthe terminal, allowing the Agent to call `terminal/output` and other methods.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Terminals](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals)",
244:                   "title": "ReleaseTerminalRequest"
245:                 },
246:                 {
247:                   "allOf": [
248:                     {
249:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/WaitForTerminalExitRequest"
250:                     }
251:                   ],
252:                   "description": "Waits for the terminal command to exit and return its exit status\n\nSee protocol docs: [Terminals](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals)",
253:                   "title": "WaitForTerminalExitRequest"
254:                 },
255:                 {
256:                   "allOf": [
257:                     {
258:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/KillTerminalRequest"
259:                     }
260:                   ],
261:                   "description": "Kills the terminal command without releasing the terminal\n\nWhile `terminal/release` will also kill the command, this method will keep\nthe `TerminalId` valid so it can be used with other methods.\n\nThis method can be helpful when implementing command timeouts which terminate\nthe command as soon as elapsed, and then get the final output so it can be sent\nto the model.\n\nNote: Call `terminal/release` when `TerminalId` is no longer needed.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Terminals](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals)",
262:                   "title": "KillTerminalRequest"
263:                 },
264:                 {
265:                   "allOf": [
266:                     {
267:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/ElicitationRequest"
268:                     }
269:                   ],
270:                   "description": "**UNSTABLE**\n\nThis capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.\n\nRequests structured user input via a form or URL.",
271:                   "title": "ElicitationRequest"
272:                 },
273:                 {
274:                   "allOf": [
275:                     {
276:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/ExtRequest"
277:                     }
278:                   ],
279:                   "description": "Handles extension method requests from the agent.\n\nAllows the Agent to send an arbitrary request that is not part of the ACP spec.\nExtension methods provide a way to add custom functionality while maintaining\nprotocol compatibility.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
280:                   "title": "ExtMethodRequest"
281:                 }
282:               ],
283:               "description": "All possible requests that an agent can send to a client.\n\nThis enum is used internally for routing RPC requests. You typically won't need\nto use this directly - instead, use the methods on the [`Client`] trait.\n\nThis enum encompasses all method calls from agent to client."
284:             },
285:             {
286:               "type": "null"
287:             }
288:           ]
289:         }
290:       },
291:       "required": ["id", "method"],
292:       "type": "object",
293:       "x-docs-ignore": true
294:     },

389:                   ],

391:                 },

395:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/PromptResponse"
396:                     }
397:                   ],
398:                   "title": "PromptResponse"
399:                 },
400:                 {
401:                   "allOf": [
402:                     {
403:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/SetSessionModelResponse"
404:                     }
405:                   ],
406:                   "title": "SetSessionModelResponse"
407:                 },
408:                 {
409:                   "allOf": [
410:                     {
411:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/StartNesResponse"
412:                     }
413:                   ],
414:                   "title": "StartNesResponse"
415:                 },

418:                     {
419:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/SuggestNesResponse"
420:                     }
421:                   ],
422:                   "title": "SuggestNesResponse"
423:                 },
424:                 {

426:                     {
427:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/CloseNesResponse"
428:                     }
429:                   ],
430:                   "title": "CloseNesResponse"
431:                 },
432:                 {

434:                     {
435:                       "$ref": "#/$defs/ExtResponse"
436:                     }
437:                   ],
438:                   "title": "ExtMethodResponse"
439:                 }
440:               ],
441:               "description": "All possible responses that an agent can send to a client.\n\nThis enum is used internally for routing RPC responses. You typically won't need\nto use this directly - the responses are handled automatically by the connection.\n\nThese are responses to the corresponding `ClientRequest` variants."
442:             }
443:           },
444:           "required": ["id", "result"],
445:           "title": "Result",
446:           "type": "object"
447:         },
448:         {

452:             },

455:             }
456:           },
457:           "required": ["id", "error"],
458:           "title": "Error",
459:           "type": "object"
460:         }
461:       ],
462:       "x-docs-ignore": true
463:     },
464:     "Annotations": {
465:       "description": "Optional annotations for the client. The client can use annotations to inform how objects are used or displayed",
466:       "properties": {
467:         "_meta": {
468:           "additionalProperties": true,
469:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
470:           "type": ["object", "null"]
471:         },
472:         "audience": {
473:           "items": {
474:             "$ref": "#/$defs/Role"
475:           },
476:           "type": ["array", "null"]
477:         },
478:         "lastModified": {
479:           "type": ["string", "null"]
480:         },
481:         "priority": {
482:           "format": "double",
483:           "type": ["number", "null"]
484:         }
485:       },
486:       "type": "object"
487:     },
488:     "AudioContent": {
489:       "description": "Audio provided to or from an LLM.",
490:       "properties": {
491:         "_meta": {
492:           "additionalProperties": true,
493:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
494:           "type": ["object", "null"]
495:         },
496:         "annotations": {
497:           "anyOf": [
498:             {
499:               "$ref": "#/$defs/Annotations"
500:             },
501:             {
502:               "type": "null"
503:             }
504:           ]
505:         },
506:         "data": {
507:           "type": "string"
508:         },
509:         "mimeType": {
510:           "type": "string"
511:         }
512:       },
513:       "required": ["data", "mimeType"],
514:       "type": "object"
515:     },
516:     "AuthCapabilities": {
517:       "description": "**UNSTABLE**\n\nThis capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.\n\nAuthentication capabilities supported by the client.\n\nAdvertised during initialization to inform the agent which authentication\nmethod types the client can handle. This governs opt-in types that require\nadditional client-side support.",
518:       "properties": {
519:         "_meta": {
520:           "additionalProperties": true,
521:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
522:           "type": ["object", "null"]
523:         },
524:         "terminal": {
525:           "default": false,
526:           "description": "Whether the client supports `terminal` authentication methods.\n\nWhen `true`, the agent may include `terminal` entries in its authentication methods.",
527:           "type": "boolean"
528:         }
529:       },
530:       "type": "object"
531:     },
532:     "AuthEnvVar": {
533:       "description": "**UNSTABLE**\n\nThis capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.\n\nDescribes a single environment variable for an [`AuthMethodEnvVar`] authentication method.",
534:       "properties": {
535:         "_meta": {
536:           "additionalProperties": true,
537:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
538:           "type": ["object", "null"]
539:         },
540:         "label": {
541:           "description": "Human-readable label for this variable, displayed in client UI.",
542:           "type": ["string", "null"]
543:         },
544:         "name": {
545:           "description": "The environment variable name (e.g. `\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"`).",
546:           "type": "string"
547:         },
548:         "optional": {
549:           "default": false,
550:           "description": "Whether this variable is optional.\n\nDefaults to `false`.",
551:           "type": "boolean"
552:         },
553:         "secret": {
554:           "default": true,
555:           "description": "Whether this value is a secret (e.g. API key, token).\nClients should use a password-style input for secret vars.\n\nDefaults to `true`.",
556:           "type": "boolean"
557:         }
558:       },
559:       "required": ["name"],
560:       "type": "object"
561:     },
562:     "AuthMethod": {
563:       "anyOf": [
564:         {
565:           "allOf": [
566:             {
567:               "$ref": "#/$defs/AuthMethodEnvVar"
568:             }
569:           ],
570:           "description": "**UNSTABLE**\n\nThis capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.\n\nUser provides a key that the client passes to the agent as an environment variable.",
571:           "properties": {
572:             "type": {
573:               "const": "env_var",
574:               "type": "string"
575:             }
576:           },
577:           "required": ["type"],
578:           "type": "object"
579:         },
580:         {
581:           "allOf": [
582:             {
583:               "$ref": "#/$defs/AuthMethodTerminal"
584:             }
585:           ],
586:           "description": "**UNSTABLE**\n\nThis capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.\n\nClient runs an interactive terminal for the user to authenticate via a TUI.",
587:           "properties": {
588:             "type": {
589:               "const": "terminal",
590:               "type": "string"
591:             }
592:           },
593:           "required": ["type"],
594:           "type": "object"
595:         },
596:         {
597:           "allOf": [
598:             {
599:               "$ref": "#/$defs/AuthMethodAgent"
600:             }
601:           ],
602:           "description": "Agent handles authentication itself.\n\nThis is the default when no `type` is specified.",
603:           "title": "agent"
604:         }
605:       ],
606:       "description": "Describes an available authentication method.\n\nThe `type` field acts as the discriminator in the serialized JSON form.\nWhen no `type` is present, the method is treated as `agent`."
607:     },
608:     "AuthMethodAgent": {
609:       "description": "Agent handles authentication itself.\n\nThis is the default authentication method type.",
610:       "properties": {
611:         "_meta": {
612:           "additionalProperties": true,
613:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
614:           "type": ["object", "null"]
615:         },
616:         "description": {
617:           "description": "Optional description providing more details about this authentication method.",
618:           "type": ["string", "null"]
619:         },
620:         "id": {
621:           "description": "Unique identifier for this authentication method.",
622:           "type": "string"
623:         },
624:         "name": {
625:           "description": "Human-readable name of the authentication method.",
626:           "type": "string"
627:         }
628:       },
629:       "required": ["id", "name"],
630:       "type": "object"
631:     },
632:     "AuthMethodEnvVar": {
633:       "description": "**UNSTABLE**\n\nThis capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.\n\nEnvironment variable authentication method.\n\nThe user provides credentials that the client passes to the agent as environment variables.",
634:       "properties": {
635:         "_meta": {
636:           "additionalProperties": true,
637:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
638:           "type": ["object", "null"]
639:         },
640:         "description": {
641:           "description": "Optional description providing more details about this authentication method.",
642:           "type": ["string", "null"]
643:         },
644:         "id": {
645:           "description": "Unique identifier for this authentication method.",
646:           "type": "string"
647:         },
648:         "link": {
649:           "description": "Optional link to a page where the user can obtain their credentials.",
650:           "type": ["string", "null"]
651:         },
652:         "name": {
653:           "description": "Human-readable name of the authentication method.",
654:           "type": "string"
655:         },
656:         "vars": {
657:           "description": "The environment variables the client should set.",
658:           "items": {
659:             "$ref": "#/$defs/AuthEnvVar"
660:           },
661:           "type": "array"
662:         }
663:       },
664:       "required": ["id", "name", "vars"],
665:       "type": "object"
666:     },
667:     "AuthMethodTerminal": {
668:       "description": "**UNSTABLE**\n\nThis capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.\n\nTerminal-based authentication method.\n\nThe client runs an interactive terminal for the user to authenticate via a TUI.",
669:       "properties": {
670:         "_meta": {
671:           "additionalProperties": true,
672:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
673:           "type": ["object", "null"]
674:         },
675:         "args": {
676:           "description": "Additional arguments to pass when running the agent binary for terminal auth.",
677:           "items": {
678:             "type": "string"
679:           },
680:           "type": "array"
681:         },
682:         "description": {
683:           "description": "Optional description providing more details about this authentication method.",
684:           "type": ["string", "null"]
685:         },
686:         "env": {
687:           "additionalProperties": {
688:             "type": "string"
689:           },
690:           "description": "Additional environment variables to set when running the agent binary for terminal auth.",
691:           "type": "object"
692:         },
693:         "id": {
694:           "description": "Unique identifier for this authentication method.",
695:           "type": "string"
696:         },
697:         "name": {
698:           "description": "Human-readable name of the authentication method.",
699:           "type": "string"
700:         }
701:       },
702:       "required": ["id", "name"],
703:       "type": "object"
704:     },
705:     "AuthenticateRequest": {
706:       "description": "Request parameters for the authenticate method.\n\nSpecifies which authentication method to use.",
707:       "properties": {
708:         "_meta": {
709:           "additionalProperties": true,
710:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
711:           "type": ["object", "null"]
712:         },
713:         "methodId": {
714:           "description": "The ID of the authentication method to use.\nMust be one of the methods advertised in the initialize response.",
715:           "type": "string"
716:         }
717:       },
718:       "required": ["methodId"],
719:       "type": "object",
720:       "x-method": "authenticate",
721:       "x-side": "agent"
722:     },
723:     "AuthenticateResponse": {
724:       "description": "Response to the `authenticate` method.",
725:       "properties": {
726:         "_meta": {
727:           "additionalProperties": true,
728:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
729:           "type": ["object", "null"]
730:         }
731:       },
732:       "type": "object",
733:       "x-method": "authenticate",
734:       "x-side": "agent"
735:     },
736:     "AvailableCommand": {
737:       "description": "Information about a command.",
738:       "properties": {
739:         "_meta": {
740:           "additionalProperties": true,
741:           "description": "The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional\nmetadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at\nthese keys.\n\nSee protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)",
742:           "type": ["object", "null"]
743:         },
744:         "description": {
745:           "description": "Human-readable description of what the command does.",
746:           "type": "string"
747:         },
748:         "input": {
749:           "anyOf": [
750:             {
751:               "$ref": "#/$defs/AvailableCommandInput"
752:             },
753:             {
754:               "type": "null"
755:             }
756:           ],
757:           "description": "Input for the command if required"
758:         },
759:         "name": {
760:           "description": "Command name (e.g., `create_plan`, `research_codebase`).",
761:           "type": "string"
762:         }

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 03:46:15)

Now I understand! The ACP protocol has `SessionModelState` and `SessionModeState` with:
- `availableModels` / `availableModes` - list of available options
- `currentModelId` / `currentModeId` - the currently selected one

Let me search for SetSessionModel and SetSessionMode to understand how to change them.

{

{
  currentModeId: string,
  availableModes: SessionMode[]
}
```

- `session/set_model` (line 5894) - `SetSessionModelRequest` with `sessionId` and `modelId`

```typescript
{
  modelId: string,
  name: string,
  description?: string
}
```

```typescript
{
  id: string,
  name: string,

33:      * Handles session update notifications from the agent.

35:      * This is a notification endpoint (no response expected) that sends
36:      * real-time updates about session progress, including message chunks,
37:      * tool calls, and execution plans.
38:      *
39:      * Note: Clients SHOULD continue accepting tool call updates even after
40:      * sending a `session/cancel` notification, as the agent may send final
41:      * updates before responding with the cancelled stop reason.
42:      *
43:      * See protocol docs: [Agent Reports Output](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#3-agent-reports-output)
44:      */
45:     sessionUpdate(params: schema.SessionNotification): Promise<void>;
46:     /**
47:      * Requests permission from the user for a tool call operation.
48:      *
49:      * Called by the agent when it needs user authorization before executing
50:      * a potentially sensitive operation. The client should present the options
51:      * to the user and return their decision.
52:      *
53:      * If the client cancels the prompt turn via `session/cancel`, it MUST
54:      * respond to this request with `RequestPermissionOutcome::Cancelled`.
55:      *
56:      * See protocol docs: [Requesting Permission](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#requesting-permission)
57:      */

59:     /**

64:      *
65:      * See protocol docs: [Client](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/overview#client)
66:      */
67:     readTextFile(params: schema.ReadTextFileRequest): Promise<schema.ReadTextFileResponse>;
68:     /**
69:      * Writes content to a text file in the client's file system.
70:      *
71:      * Only available if the client advertises the `fs.writeTextFile` capability.

73:      *
74:      * See protocol docs: [Client](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/overview#client)
75:      */
76:     writeTextFile(params: schema.WriteTextFileRequest): Promise<schema.WriteTextFileResponse>;
77:     /**
78:      * Executes a command in a new terminal.
79:      *
80:      * Returns a `TerminalHandle` that can be used to get output, wait for exit,
81:      * kill the command, or release the terminal.
82:      *
83:      * The terminal can also be embedded in tool calls by using its ID in
84:      * `ToolCallContent` with type "terminal".

88:      */

94:      */

101:     extNotification(method: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>;
102:     /**
103:      * AbortSignal that aborts when the connection closes.
104:      *
105:      * This signal can be used to:
106:      * - Listen for connection closure: `connection.signal.addEventListener('abort', () => {...})`
107:      * - Check connection status synchronously: `if (connection.signal.aborted) {...}`
108:      * - Pass to other APIs (fetch, setTimeout) for automatic cancellation
109:      *
110:      * The connection closes when the underlying stream ends, either normally or due to an error.
111:      *
112:      * @example
113:      * ```typescript
114:      * const connection = new AgentSideConnection(agent, stream);
115:      *
116:      * // Listen for closure
117:      * connection.signal.addEventListener('abort', () => {
118:      *   console.log('Connection closed - performing cleanup');
119:      * });
120:      *
121:      * // Check status
122:      * if (connection.signal.aborted) {
123:      *   console.log('Connection is already closed');
124:      * }
125:      *
126:      * // Pass to other APIs
127:      * fetch(url, { signal: connection.signal });
128:      * ```
129:      */

131:     /**
132:      * Promise that resolves when the connection closes.
133:      *
134:      * The connection closes when the underlying stream ends, either normally or due to an error.

136:      *
137:      * This is useful for async/await style cleanup:
138:      *
139:      * @example
140:      * ```typescript
141:      * const connection = new AgentSideConnection(agent, stream);
142:      * await connection.closed;

148: /**
149:  * Handle for controlling and monitoring a terminal created via `createTerminal`.
150:  *
151:  * Provides methods to:
152:  * - Get current output without waiting
153:  * - Wait for command completion
154:  * - Kill the running command
155:  * - Release terminal resources
156:  *
157:  * **Important:** Always call `release()` when done with the terminal to free resources.
158: 
159:  * The terminal supports async disposal via `Symbol.asyncDispose` for automatic cleanup.
160: 
161:  * You can use `await using` to ensure the terminal is automatically released when it
162:  * goes out of scope.
163:  */

174:      */

176:     /**
177:      * Kills the terminal command without releasing the terminal.
178:      *
179:      * The terminal remains valid after killing, allowing you to:
180:      * - Get the final output with `currentOutput()`

187:     /**
188:      * Releases the terminal and frees all associated resources.

190:      * If the command is still running, it will be killed.
191:      * After release, the terminal ID becomes invalid and cannot be used
192:      * with other terminal methods.
193:      *
194:      * Tool calls that already reference this terminal will continue to
195:      * display its output.
196:      *
197:      * **Important:** Always call this method when done with the terminal.
198:      */
199:     release(): Promise<schema.ReleaseTerminalResponse | void>;

201: }
202: /**
203:  * A client-side connection to an agent.
204:  *
205:  * This class provides the client's view of an ACP connection, allowing
206:  * clients (such as code editors) to communicate with agents. It implements
207:  * the {@link Agent} interface to provide methods for initializing sessions, sending
208:  * prompts, and managing the agent lifecycle.

220:      * @param toClient - A function that creates a Client handler to process incoming agent requests

224:      * See protocol docs: [Communication Model](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/overview#communication-model)
225:      */
226:     constructor(toClient: (agent: Agent) => Client, stream: Stream);
227:     /**
228:      * Establishes the connection with a client and negotiates protocol capabilities.
229:      *
230:      * This method is called once at the beginning of the connection to:
231:      * - Negotiate the protocol version to use
232:      * - Exchange capability information between client and agent
233:      * - Determine available authentication methods
234:      *
235:      * The agent should respond with its supported protocol version and capabilities.
236:      *
237:      * See protocol docs: [Initialization](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization)
238:      */
239:     initialize(params: schema.InitializeRequest): Promise<schema.InitializeResponse>;
240:     /**

242:      *
243:      * Sessions represent independent conversation contexts with their own history and state.
244:      *
245:      * The agent should:
246:      * - Create a new session context
247:      * - Connect to any specified MCP servers
248:      * - Return a unique session ID for future requests
249:      *
250:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to expand the session's filesystem
251:      * scope beyond `cwd` without changing the base for relative paths.
252:      *
253:      * May return an `auth_required` error if the agent requires authentication.
254:      *
255:      * See protocol docs: [Session Setup](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-setup)
256:      */
257:     newSession(params: schema.NewSessionRequest): Promise<schema.NewSessionResponse>;
258:     /**
259:      * Loads an existing session to resume a previous conversation.
260:      *
261:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `loadSession` capability.
262:      *
263:      * The agent should:
264:      * - Restore the session context and conversation history
265:      * - Connect to the specified MCP servers
266:      * - Stream the entire conversation history back to the client via notifications
267:      *
268:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to set the complete list of
269:      * additional workspace roots for the loaded session.
270:      *
271:      * See protocol docs: [Loading Sessions](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-setup#loading-sessions)
272:      */
273:     loadSession(params: schema.LoadSessionRequest): Promise<schema.LoadSessionResponse>;
274:     /**
275:      * **UNSTABLE**
276:      *
277:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
278:      *
279:      * Forks an existing session to create a new independent session.
280:      *
281:      * Creates a new session based on the context of an existing one, allowing
282:      * operations like generating summaries without affecting the original session's history.
283:      *
284:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to set the complete list of
285:      * additional workspace roots for the forked session.
286:      *
287:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `session.fork` capability.
288:      *
289:      * @experimental
290:      */
291:     unstable_forkSession(params: schema.ForkSessionRequest): Promise<schema.ForkSessionResponse>;
292:     /**
293:      * Lists existing sessions from the agent.
294:      *
295:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `listSessions` capability.
296:      *
297:      * Returns a list of sessions with metadata like session ID, working directory,
298:      * title, and last update time. Supports filtering by working directory,
299:      * `additionalDirectories`, and cursor-based pagination.
300:      */
301:     listSessions(params: schema.ListSessionsRequest): Promise<schema.ListSessionsResponse>;
302:     /**
303:      * **UNSTABLE**
304:      *
305:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
306:      *
307:      * Resumes an existing session without returning previous messages.
308:      *
309:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `session.resume` capability.
310:      *
311:      * The agent should resume the session context, allowing the conversation to continue
312:      * without replaying the message history (unlike `session/load`).
313:      *
314:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to set the complete list of
315:      * additional workspace roots for the resumed session.
316:      *
317:      * @experimental
318:      */
319:     unstable_resumeSession(params: schema.ResumeSessionRequest): Promise<schema.ResumeSessionResponse>;
320:     /**
321:      * **UNSTABLE**
322:      *
323:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
324:      *
325:      * Closes an active session and frees up any resources associated with it.
326:      *
327:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `session.close` capability.
328:      *
329:      * The agent must cancel any ongoing work (as if `session/cancel` was called)
330:      * and then free up any resources associated with the session.
331:      *
332:      * @experimental
333:      */
334:     unstable_closeSession(params: schema.CloseSessionRequest): Promise<schema.CloseSessionResponse>;
335:     /**
336:      * Sets the operational mode for a session.
337:      *
338:      * Allows switching between different agent modes (e.g., "ask", "architect", "code")
339:      * that affect system prompts, tool availability, and permission behaviors.
340:      *
341:      * The mode must be one of the modes advertised in `availableModes` during session
342:      * creation or loading. Agents may also change modes autonomously and notify the
343:      * client via `current_mode_update` notifications.
344:      *
345:      * This method can be called at any time during a session, whether the Agent is
346:      * idle or actively generating a turn.
347:      *
348:      * See protocol docs: [Session Modes](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-modes)
349:      */
350:     setSessionMode(params: schema.SetSessionModeRequest): Promise<schema.SetSessionModeResponse>;
351:     /**
352:      * **UNSTABLE**
353:      *
354:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
355:      *
356:      * Select a model for a given session.
357:      *
358:      * @experimental
359:      */
360:     unstable_setSessionModel(params: schema.SetSessionModelRequest): Promise<schema.SetSessionModelResponse>;
361:     /**
362:      * Set a configuration option for a given session.
363:      *
364:      * The response contains the full set of configuration options and their current values,
365:      * as changing one option may affect the available values or state of other options.
366:      */
367:     setSessionConfigOption(params: schema.SetSessionConfigOptionRequest): Promise<schema.SetSessionConfigOptionResponse>;
368:     /**
369:      * Authenticates the client using the specified authentication method.
370:      *
371:      * Called when the agent requires authentication before allowing session creation.
372:      * The client provides the authentication method ID that was advertised during initialization.
373:      *
374:      * After successful authentication, the client can proceed to create sessions with

376:      *
377:      * See protocol docs: [Initialization](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization)
378:      */

380:     /**

385:      * @experimental
386:      */
387:     unstable_logout(params: schema.LogoutRequest): Promise<schema.LogoutResponse>;
388:     /**
389:      * Processes a user prompt within a session.
390:      *
391:      * This method handles the whole lifecycle of a prompt:
392:      * - Receives user messages with optional context (files, images, etc.)

394:      * - Reports language model content and tool calls to the Clients

396:      * - Executes any requested tool calls

406:      *
407:      * Upon receiving this notification, the Agent SHOULD:
408:      * - Stop all language model requests as soon as possible
409:      * - Abort all tool call invocations in progress
410:      * - Send any pending `session/update` notifications
411:      * - Respond to the original `session/prompt` request with `StopReason::Cancelled`
412:      *

414:      */

422:      */

424:     /**
425:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
426:      *
427:      * Sends a NES suggestion request.
428:      *
429:      * @experimental
430:      */
431:     unstable_suggestNes(params: schema.SuggestNesRequest): Promise<schema.SuggestNesResponse>;
432:     /**
433:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
434:      *
435:      * Closes a NES session.
436:      *
437:      * @experimental
438:      */
439:     unstable_closeNes(params: schema.CloseNesRequest): Promise<schema.CloseNesResponse>;
440:     /**
