442:      *
443:      * Notifies the agent that a document was opened.
444:      *
445:      * @experimental
446:      */
447:     unstable_didOpenDocument(params: schema.DidOpenDocumentNotification): Promise<void>;
448:     /**
449:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
450:      *
451:      * Notifies the agent that a document was changed.
452:      *
453:      * @experimental
454:      */
455:     unstable_didChangeDocument(params: schema.DidChangeDocumentNotification): Promise<void>;
456:     /**
457:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
458:      *
459:      * Notifies the agent that a document was closed.
460:      *
461:      * @experimental
462:      */
463:     unstable_didCloseDocument(params: schema.DidCloseDocumentNotification): Promise<void>;
464:     /**
465:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
466:      *
467:      * Notifies the agent that a document was saved.
468:      *
469:      * @experimental
470:      */
471:     unstable_didSaveDocument(params: schema.DidSaveDocumentNotification): Promise<void>;
472:     /**
473:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
474:      *
475:      * Notifies the agent that a document received focus.
476:      *
477:      * @experimental
478:      */
479:     unstable_didFocusDocument(params: schema.DidFocusDocumentNotification): Promise<void>;
480:     /**
481:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
482:      *
483:      * Notifies the agent that a NES suggestion was accepted.
484:      *
485:      * @experimental
486:      */
487:     unstable_acceptNes(params: schema.AcceptNesNotification): Promise<void>;
488:     /**
489:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
490:      *
491:      * Notifies the agent that a NES suggestion was rejected.
492:      *
493:      * @experimental
494:      */
495:     unstable_rejectNes(params: schema.RejectNesNotification): Promise<void>;
496:     /**
497:      * Extension method
498:      *
499:      * Allows the Client to send an arbitrary request that is not part of the ACP spec.
500:      */
501:     extMethod(method: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<Record<string, unknown>>;
502:     /**
503:      * Extension notification
504:      *
505:      * Allows the Client to send an arbitrary notification that is not part of the ACP spec.
506:      */
507:     extNotification(method: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>;
508:     /**
509:      * AbortSignal that aborts when the connection closes.
510:      *
511:      * This signal can be used to:
512:      * - Listen for connection closure: `connection.signal.addEventListener('abort', () => {...})`
513:      * - Check connection status synchronously: `if (connection.signal.aborted) {...}`
514:      * - Pass to other APIs (fetch, setTimeout) for automatic cancellation
515:      *
516:      * The connection closes when the underlying stream ends, either normally or due to an error.
517:      *
518:      * @example
519:      * ```typescript
520:      * const connection = new ClientSideConnection(client, stream);
521:      *
522:      * // Listen for closure
523:      * connection.signal.addEventListener('abort', () => {
524:      *   console.log('Connection closed - performing cleanup');
525:      * });
526:      *
527:      * // Check status
528:      * if (connection.signal.aborted) {
529:      *   console.log('Connection is already closed');
530:      * }
531:      *
532:      * // Pass to other APIs
533:      * fetch(url, { signal: connection.signal });
534:      * ```
535:      */
536:     get signal(): AbortSignal;
537:     /**
538:      * Promise that resolves when the connection closes.
539:      *
540:      * The connection closes when the underlying stream ends, either normally or due to an error.
541:      * Once closed, the connection cannot send or receive any more messages.
542:      *
543:      * This is useful for async/await style cleanup:
544:      *
545:      * @example
546:      * ```typescript
547:      * const connection = new ClientSideConnection(client, stream);
548:      * await connection.closed;
549:      * console.log('Connection closed - performing cleanup');
550:      * ```
551:      */
552:     get closed(): Promise<void>;
553: }
554: export type { AnyMessage } from "./jsonrpc.js";
555: declare class Connection {
556:     #private;
557:     constructor(requestHandler: RequestHandler, notificationHandler: NotificationHandler, stream: Stream);
558:     /**
559:      * AbortSignal that aborts when the connection closes.
560:      *
561:      * This signal can be used to:
562:      * - Listen for connection closure via event listeners
563:      * - Check connection status synchronously with `signal.aborted`
564:      * - Pass to other APIs (fetch, setTimeout) for automatic cancellation
565:      */
566:     get signal(): AbortSignal;
567:     /**
568:      * Promise that resolves when the connection closes.
569:      *
570:      * The connection closes when the underlying stream ends, either normally
571:      * or due to an error. Once closed, the connection cannot send or receive
572:      * any more messages.
573:      *
574:      * @example
575:      * ```typescript
576:      * const connection = new ClientSideConnection(client, stream);
577:      * await connection.closed;
578:      * console.log('Connection closed - performing cleanup');
579:      * ```
580:      */
581:     get closed(): Promise<void>;
582:     sendRequest<Req, Resp>(method: string, params?: Req): Promise<Resp>;
583:     sendNotification<N>(method: string, params?: N): Promise<void>;
584: }
585: /**
586:  * JSON-RPC error object.
587:  *
588:  * Represents an error that occurred during method execution, following the
589:  * JSON-RPC 2.0 error object specification with optional additional data.
590:  *
591:  * See protocol docs: [JSON-RPC Error Object](https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#error_object)
592:  */
593: export declare class RequestError extends Error {
594:     code: number;
595:     data?: unknown;
596:     constructor(code: number, message: string, data?: unknown);
597:     /**
598:      * Invalid JSON was received by the server. An error occurred on the server while parsing the JSON text.
599:      */
600:     static parseError(data?: unknown, additionalMessage?: string): RequestError;
601:     /**
602:      * The JSON sent is not a valid Request object.
603:      */
604:     static invalidRequest(data?: unknown, additionalMessage?: string): RequestError;
605:     /**
606:      * The method does not exist / is not available.
607:      */
608:     static methodNotFound(method: string): RequestError;
609:     /**
610:      * Invalid method parameter(s).
611:      */
612:     static invalidParams(data?: unknown, additionalMessage?: string): RequestError;
613:     /**
614:      * Internal JSON-RPC error.
615:      */
616:     static internalError(data?: unknown, additionalMessage?: string): RequestError;
617:     /**
618:      * Authentication required.
619:      */
620:     static authRequired(data?: unknown, additionalMessage?: string): RequestError;
621:     /**
622:      * Resource, such as a file, was not found
623:      */
624:     static resourceNotFound(uri?: string): RequestError;
625:     toResult<T>(): Result<T>;
626:     toErrorResponse(): ErrorResponse;
627: }
628: /**
629:  * The Client interface defines the interface that ACP-compliant clients must implement.
630:  *
631:  * Clients are typically code editors (IDEs, text editors) that provide the interface
632:  * between users and AI agents. They manage the environment, handle user interactions,
633:  * and control access to resources.
634:  */
635: export interface Client {
636:     /**
637:      * Requests permission from the user for a tool call operation.
638:      *
639:      * Called by the agent when it needs user authorization before executing
640:      * a potentially sensitive operation. The client should present the options
641:      * to the user and return their decision.
642:      *
643:      * If the client cancels the prompt turn via `session/cancel`, it MUST
644:      * respond to this request with `RequestPermissionOutcome::Cancelled`.
645:      *
646:      * See protocol docs: [Requesting Permission](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#requesting-permission)
647:      */
648:     requestPermission(params: schema.RequestPermissionRequest): Promise<schema.RequestPermissionResponse>;
649:     /**
650:      * Handles session update notifications from the agent.
651:      *
652:      * This is a notification endpoint (no response expected) that receives
653:      * real-time updates about session progress, including message chunks,
654:      * tool calls, and execution plans.
655:      *
656:      * Note: Clients SHOULD continue accepting tool call updates even after
657:      * sending a `session/cancel` notification, as the agent may send final
658:      * updates before responding with the cancelled stop reason.
659:      *
660:      * See protocol docs: [Agent Reports Output](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#3-agent-reports-output)
661:      */
662:     sessionUpdate(params: schema.SessionNotification): Promise<void>;
663:     /**
664:      * Writes content to a text file in the client's file system.
665:      *
666:      * Only available if the client advertises the `fs.writeTextFile` capability.
667:      * Allows the agent to create or modify files within the client's environment.
668:      *
669:      * See protocol docs: [Client](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/overview#client)
670:      */
671:     writeTextFile?(params: schema.WriteTextFileRequest): Promise<schema.WriteTextFileResponse>;
672:     /**
673:      * Reads content from a text file in the client's file system.
674:      *
675:      * Only available if the client advertises the `fs.readTextFile` capability.
676:      * Allows the agent to access file contents within the client's environment.
677:      *
678:      * See protocol docs: [Client](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/overview#client)
679:      */
680:     readTextFile?(params: schema.ReadTextFileRequest): Promise<schema.ReadTextFileResponse>;
681:     /**
682:      * Creates a new terminal to execute a command.
683:      *
684:      * Only available if the `terminal` capability is set to `true`.
685:      *
686:      * The Agent must call `releaseTerminal` when done with the terminal
687:      * to free resources.
688:   
689:      * @see {@link https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals | Terminal Documentation}
690:      */
691:     createTerminal?(params: schema.CreateTerminalRequest): Promise<schema.CreateTerminalResponse>;
692:     /**
693:      * Gets the current output and exit status of a terminal.
694:      *
695:      * Returns immediately without waiting for the command to complete.
696:      * If the command has already exited, the exit status is included.
697:      *
698:      * @see {@link https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals#getting-output | Getting Terminal Output}
699:      */
700:     terminalOutput?(params: schema.TerminalOutputRequest): Promise<schema.TerminalOutputResponse>;
701:     /**
702:      * Releases a terminal and frees all associated resources.
703:      *
704:      * The command is killed if it hasn't exited yet. After release,
705:      * the terminal ID becomes invalid for all other terminal methods.
706:      *
707:      * Tool calls that already contain the terminal ID continue to
708:      * display its output.
709:      *
710:      * @see {@link https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals#releasing-terminals | Releasing Terminals}
711:      */
712:     releaseTerminal?(params: schema.ReleaseTerminalRequest): Promise<schema.ReleaseTerminalResponse | void>;
713:     /**
714:      * Waits for a terminal command to exit and returns its exit status.
715:      *
716:      * This method returns once the command completes, providing the
717:      * exit code and/or signal that terminated the process.
718:      *
719:      * @see {@link https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals#waiting-for-exit | Waiting for Exit}
720:      */
721:     waitForTerminalExit?(params: schema.WaitForTerminalExitRequest): Promise<schema.WaitForTerminalExitResponse>;
722:     /**
723:      * Kills a terminal command without releasing the terminal.
724:      *
725:      * While `releaseTerminal` also kills the command, this method keeps
726:      * the terminal ID valid so it can be used with other methods.
727:      *
728:      * Useful for implementing command timeouts that terminate the command
729:      * and then retrieve the final output.
730:      *
731:      * Note: Call `releaseTerminal` when the terminal is no longer needed.
732:      *
733:      * @see {@link https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals#killing-commands | Killing Commands}
734:      */
735:     killTerminal?(params: schema.KillTerminalRequest): Promise<schema.KillTerminalResponse | void>;
736:     /**
737:      * Extension method
738:      *
739:      * Allows the Agent to send an arbitrary request that is not part of the ACP spec.
740:      *
741:      * To help avoid conflicts, it's a good practice to prefix extension
742:      * methods with a unique identifier such as domain name.
743:      */
744:     extMethod?(method: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<Record<string, unknown>>;
745:     /**
746:      * Extension notification
747:      *
748:      * Allows the Agent to send an arbitrary notification that is not part of the ACP spec.
749:      */
750:     extNotification?(method: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>;
751: }
752: /**
753:  * The Agent interface defines the interface that all ACP-compliant agents must implement.
754:  *
755:  * Agents are programs that use generative AI to autonomously modify code. They handle
756:  * requests from clients and execute tasks using language models and tools.
757:  */
758: export interface Agent {
759:     /**
760:      * Establishes the connection with a client and negotiates protocol capabilities.
761:      *
762:      * This method is called once at the beginning of the connection to:
763:      * - Negotiate the protocol version to use
764:      * - Exchange capability information between client and agent
765:      * - Determine available authentication methods
766:      *
767:      * The agent should respond with its supported protocol version and capabilities.
768:      *
769:      * See protocol docs: [Initialization](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization)
770:      */
771:     initialize(params: schema.InitializeRequest): Promise<schema.InitializeResponse>;
772:     /**
773:      * Creates a new conversation session with the agent.
774:      *
775:      * Sessions represent independent conversation contexts with their own history and state.
776:      *
777:      * The agent should:
778:      * - Create a new session context
779:      * - Connect to any specified MCP servers
780:      * - Return a unique session ID for future requests
781:      *
782:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to expand the session's filesystem
783:      * scope beyond `cwd` without changing the base for relative paths.
784:      *
785:      * May return an `auth_required` error if the agent requires authentication.
786:      *
787:      * See protocol docs: [Session Setup](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-setup)
788:      */
789:     newSession(params: schema.NewSessionRequest): Promise<schema.NewSessionResponse>;
790:     /**
791:      * Loads an existing session to resume a previous conversation.
792:      *
793:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `loadSession` capability.
794:      *
795:      * The agent should:
796:      * - Restore the session context and conversation history
797:      * - Connect to the specified MCP servers
798:      * - Stream the entire conversation history back to the client via notifications
799:      *
800:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to set the complete list of
801:      * additional workspace roots for the loaded session.
802:      *
803:      * See protocol docs: [Loading Sessions](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-setup#loading-sessions)
804:      */
805:     loadSession?(params: schema.LoadSessionRequest): Promise<schema.LoadSessionResponse>;
806:     /**
807:      * **UNSTABLE**
808:      *
809:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
810:      *
811:      * Forks an existing session to create a new independent session.
812:      *
813:      * Creates a new session based on the context of an existing one, allowing
814:      * operations like generating summaries without affecting the original session's history.
815:      *
816:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to set the complete list of
817:      * additional workspace roots for the forked session.
818:      *
819:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `session.fork` capability.
820:      *
821:      * @experimental
822:      */
823:     unstable_forkSession?(params: schema.ForkSessionRequest): Promise<schema.ForkSessionResponse>;
824:     /**
825:      * Lists existing sessions from the agent.
826:      *
827:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `listSessions` capability.
828:      *
829:      * Returns a list of sessions with metadata like session ID, working directory,
830:      * title, and last update time. Supports filtering by working directory,
831:      * `additionalDirectories`, and cursor-based pagination.
832:      */
833:     listSessions?(params: schema.ListSessionsRequest): Promise<schema.ListSessionsResponse>;
834:     /**
835:      * **UNSTABLE**
836:      *
837:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
838:      *
839:      * Resumes an existing session without returning previous messages.
840:      *
841:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `session.resume` capability.
842:      *
843:      * The agent should resume the session context, allowing the conversation to continue
844:      * without replaying the message history (unlike `session/load`).
845:      *
846:      * The request may include `additionalDirectories` to set the complete list of
847:      * additional workspace roots for the resumed session.
848:      *
849:      * @experimental
850:      */
851:     unstable_resumeSession?(params: schema.ResumeSessionRequest): Promise<schema.ResumeSessionResponse>;
852:     /**
853:      * **UNSTABLE**
854:      *
855:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
856:      *
857:      * Closes an active session and frees up any resources associated with it.
858:      *
859:      * This method is only available if the agent advertises the `session.close` capability.
860:      *
861:      * The agent must cancel any ongoing work (as if `session/cancel` was called)
862:      * and then free up any resources associated with the session.
863:      *
864:      * @experimental
865:      */
866:     unstable_closeSession?(params: schema.CloseSessionRequest): Promise<schema.CloseSessionResponse>;
867:     /**
868:      * Sets the operational mode for a session.
869:      *
870:      * Allows switching between different agent modes (e.g., "ask", "architect", "code")
871:      * that affect system prompts, tool availability, and permission behaviors.
872:      *
873:      * The mode must be one of the modes advertised in `availableModes` during session
874:      * creation or loading. Agents may also change modes autonomously and notify the
875:      * client via `current_mode_update` notifications.
876:      *
877:      * This method can be called at any time during a session, whether the Agent is
878:      * idle or actively generating a turn.
879:      *
880:      * See protocol docs: [Session Modes](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-modes)
881:      */
882:     setSessionMode?(params: schema.SetSessionModeRequest): Promise<schema.SetSessionModeResponse | void>;
883:     /**
884:      * **UNSTABLE**
885:      *
886:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
887:      *
888:      * Select a model for a given session.
889:      *
890:      * @experimental
891:      */
892:     unstable_setSessionModel?(params: schema.SetSessionModelRequest): Promise<schema.SetSessionModelResponse | void>;
893:     /**
894:      * Set a configuration option for a given session.
895:      *
896:      * The response contains the full set of configuration options and their current values,
897:      * as changing one option may affect the available values or state of other options.
898:      */
899:     setSessionConfigOption?(params: schema.SetSessionConfigOptionRequest): Promise<schema.SetSessionConfigOptionResponse>;
900:     /**
901:      * Authenticates the client using the specified authentication method.
902:      *
903:      * Called when the agent requires authentication before allowing session creation.
904:      * The client provides the authentication method ID that was advertised during initialization.
905:      *
906:      * After successful authentication, the client can proceed to create sessions with
907:      * `newSession` without receiving an `auth_required` error.
908:      *
909:      * See protocol docs: [Initialization](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization)
910:      */
911:     authenticate(params: schema.AuthenticateRequest): Promise<schema.AuthenticateResponse | void>;
912:     /**
913:      * Terminates the current authenticated session.
914:      *
915:      * **UNSTABLE**: This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
916:      *
917:      * @experimental
918:      */
919:     unstable_logout?(params: schema.LogoutRequest): Promise<schema.LogoutResponse | void>;
920:     /**
921:      * Processes a user prompt within a session.
922:      *
923:      * This method handles the whole lifecycle of a prompt:
924:      * - Receives user messages with optional context (files, images, etc.)
925:      * - Processes the prompt using language models
926:      * - Reports language model content and tool calls to the Clients
927:      * - Requests permission to run tools
928:      * - Executes any requested tool calls
929:      * - Returns when the turn is complete with a stop reason
930:      *
931:      * See protocol docs: [Prompt Turn](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn)
932:      */
933:     prompt(params: schema.PromptRequest): Promise<schema.PromptResponse>;
934:     /**
935:      * Cancels ongoing operations for a session.
936:      *
937:      * This is a notification sent by the client to cancel an ongoing prompt turn.
938:      *
939:      * Upon receiving this notification, the Agent SHOULD:
940:      * - Stop all language model requests as soon as possible
941:      * - Abort all tool call invocations in progress
942:      * - Send any pending `session/update` notifications
943:      * - Respond to the original `session/prompt` request with `StopReason::Cancelled`
944:      *

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 10:38:55)

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture of what the ACP SDK provides. Let me summarize what I found:

**ACP SDK provides these methods:**
1. `unstable_setSessionModel(params)` - Select a model for a given session
2. `setSessionMode(params)` - Sets the operational mode for a session

**And these types:**
1. `SessionModelState` - Contains `currentModelId` and `availableModels` (array of `ModelInfo`)
2. `SessionModeState` - Contains `currentModeId` and `availableModes` (array of `SessionMode`)
3. `ModelInfo` - Contains `modelId`, `name`, and optional `description`
4. `SessionMode` - Contains `id`, `name`, and optional `description`

**In the NewSessionResponse:**
- `models?: SessionModelState | null` - Initial model state if supported
- `modes?: SessionModeState | null` - Initial mode state if supported

So the ACP server (opencode acp) already supports:
1. Returning available models and modes when creating a session
2. Setting/changing models via `unstable_setSessionModel`
3. Setting/changing modes via `setSessionMode`

Now I need to understand the flow:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         CURRENT FLOW                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                          │
│   1. SessionDetailPage.tsx shows session header                          │
│      (no model/mode selectors)                                          │
│                                                                          │
│   2. mimo-agent/src/index.ts creates ACP session                         │
│      - Calls session.acpConnection.newSession({ cwd, mcpServers })    │
│      - Response includes models and modes (if supported)                │
│      - But mimo-agent doesn't store or forward these                      │
│                                                                          │
│   3. chat.js handles WebSocket messages                                   │
│      - No model/mode state management                                     │
│                                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Now let me think about what needs to be implemented:


I'll focus on implementing a comprehensive solution for managing model and mode states across the platform. The key integration points involve creating new UI components, updating communication protocols, and ensuring seamless state management between the frontend and backend systems.

The implementation requires careful coordination between different layers: user interface interactions, backend communication, and agent-level configuration. I'll need to design a flexible approach that allows for dynamic model and mode selection while maintaining a clean and intuitive user experience.

Key technical challenges include:
- Designing a responsive dropdown interface
- Ensuring real-time state synchronization
- Handling potential errors or unavailable states
- Maintaining backward compatibility with existing session management
## Agent (2026-04-04 10:39:04)

Perfect! Now I understand the full picture. The ACP SDK already supports everything we need:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ACP SDK CAPABILITIES                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                          │
│   SessionModelState (unstable/experimental)                               │
│   ├── currentModelId: string                                            │
│   └── availableModels: ModelInfo[]                                     │
│       └── { modelId, name, description? }                               │
│                                                                          │
│   SessionModeState                                                       │
│   ├── currentModeId: string                                             │
│   └── availableModes: SessionMode[]                                     │
│       └── { id, name, description? }                                     │
│                                                                          │
│   Methods available on ClientSideConnection:                             │
│   ├── unstable_setSessionModel({ sessionId, modelId })                  │
│   └── setSessionMode({ sessionId, modeId })                            │
│                                                                          │
│   NewSessionResponse includes:                                           │
│   ├── models?: SessionModelState | null                                 │
│   └── modes?: SessionModeState | null                                   │
│                                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Current state vs desired:**

```
CURRENT:                    DESIRED:
┌──────────────┐           ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Session UI   │           │ Session UI                               │
│              │           │ ┌────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│ No model     │           │ │ 🤖 Model: [kimik2.5 ▼]             │  │
│ selector     │           │ │ 🎯 Mode:  [explore ▼]              │  │
│              │           │ └────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└──────────────┘           └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
         │                            │
         ▼                            ▼
┌──────────────┐           ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ mimo-agent   │           │ mimo-agent                               │
│ newSession({ │           │ newSession({                             │
│   cwd,       │           │   cwd, mcpServers,                       │
│   mcpServers │           │   modelId?, modeId?  // optional initial  │
│ })           │           │ })                                       │
│              │           │                                          │
│ Ignores      │           │ Forwards to agent with selection         │
│ model/mode   │           │                                          │
│ in response  │           │ Captures models/modes from response      │
│              │           │ Forwards to platform via WebSocket       │
└──────────────┘           └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**The implementation path:**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    IMPLEMENTATION STEPS                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                          │
│  1. DATABASE (mimo-platform)                                            │
│     ├── Add modelState, modeState columns to sessions table             │
│     └── Store JSON blobs of SessionModelState/SessionModeState          │
│                                                                          │
│  2. SESSION REPOSITORY (mimo-platform)                                  │
│     ├── Update Session type to include modelState/modeState             │
│     └── Add methods to update these fields                              │
│                                                                          │
│  3. MIMO-AGENT (mimo-agent/src/index.ts)                                  │
│     ├── Capture models/modes from NewSessionResponse                     │
│     ├── Send to platform via WebSocket                                   │
│     └── Handle "set_model" / "set_mode" messages from platform          │
│                                                                          │
│  4. PLATFORM WEBSOCKET (mimo-platform)                                    │
│     ├── Store received model/mode state in session                      │
│     ├── Broadcast to UI via WebSocket                                   │
│     └── Handle UI -> agent model/mode change requests                   │
│                                                                          │
│  5. UI (SessionDetailPage.tsx + chat.js)                                  │
│     ├── Add model/mode dropdowns to session header                    │
│     ├── Populate with state from WebSocket                              │
│     └── Send change requests via WebSocket                              │
│                                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key questions to resolve:**

1. **Initial selection** - Should new sessions:
   - Use the first available model/mode by default?
   - Have no selection until user picks?
   - Use a configured default?

2. **Persistence** - When user changes model/mode:
   - Update database immediately?
   - Just in-memory and re-fetch on reload?

3. **Mimo-Agent communication** - Does mimo-agent:
   - Just forward the change to ACP?
   - Or also track current state?

This is a solid foundation. Want to dive deeper into any specific layer, or should we capture this exploration into an OpenSpec change to start implementation?

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 10:41:46)

This is very revealing! I can see:

1. `SessionConfigOption` is a polymorphic type with discriminator `type`
2. It can be a "select" (dropdown) or "boolean" (toggle)
3. There's a `SessionConfigOptionCategory` with values like:
   - `"mode"` - Session mode selector
   - `"model"` - Model selector
   - `"thought_level"` - Thought/reasoning level selector
   - `"other"` - Uncategorized

So the model and mode are treated as CONFIGURATION OPTIONS, not separate fields on the response!

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 10:41:49)

This is very interesting! The ACP schema defines `SessionConfigOption` as a union type with two variants:
1. `SessionConfigSelect` - A dropdown selector for model/mode
2. `SessionConfigBoolean` - A toggle switch

And `SessionConfigOptionCategory` includes:
- "mode" - Session mode selector
- "model" - Model selector
- "thought_level" - Thought/reasoning level selector
- "other" - Uncategorized

This means `opencode acp` might be exposing models and modes as `configOptions` with specific categories, rather than the separate `SessionModelState` and `SessionModeState` types!

Let me read more about SessionConfigSelect.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 10:41:52)

Now I understand! The `opencode acp` server is using `SessionConfigOption` with a `category` field to represent models and modes. This is actually a newer pattern in the ACP spec where instead of having separate `SessionModelState` and `SessionModeState` fields, they are unified as configuration options.

So the response from `newSession` would include:
- `configOptions`: An array of `SessionConfigOption` 
- Each option has a `category` which can be `"model"`, `"mode"`, `"thought_level"`, or `"other"`

And the options are of type `"select"` with:
- `currentValue`: The currently selected value ID
- `options`: Array of selectable values (flat list or grouped)

This is different from what I saw earlier with `SessionModelState` and `SessionModeState` - those are separate top-level fields in the response, but `opencode acp` is using the `configOptions` approach.

Let me verify this by looking at how the NewSessionResponse includes configOptions:

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 10:41:58)

Now I understand! Looking at the schema, I see:

1. `ForkSessionResponse` (and `NewSessionResponse`) includes:
   - `configOptions`: SessionConfigOption[] - These are the configuration options
   - `models`: SessionModelState - The model state (separate from configOptions)
   - `modes`: SessionModeState - The mode state (separate from configOptions)

2. `SessionConfigOption` is a generic configuration system with:
   - `type`: "select" or "boolean"
   - `category`: Can be "mode", "model", "thought_level", or "other"
   - `id`, `name`, `description`
   - For select: `currentValue` and `options`

So it seems like opencode returns models/modes in TWO places:
1. The legacy/experimental `models` and `modes` fields in the response
2. Possibly also as `configOptions` with specific categories

The user mentioned they saw models in `configOptions`. Let me check if there's a NewSessionResponse definition to see how it returns these.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 10:42:09)

Now I understand! The model/mode are returned in TWO places:

1. **Direct properties** (unstable/experimental):
   - `response.models` - SessionModelState
   - `response.modes` - SessionModeState

2. **configOptions array** (the standard/stable way):
   - `response.configOptions` - SessionConfigOption[]
   - These have categories like "mode", "model", "thought_level"
   - Type can be "select" (dropdown) or "boolean" (toggle)

So based on the user's observation, opencode acp returns them in configOptions. Let me verify NewSessionResponse:

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 10:42:20)

Perfect! Now I can see the complete picture. The ACP protocol returns `configOptions` in `NewSessionResponse`, and based on the user's mention that "models are returning in the configOptions", it appears opencode returns model/mode information via config options with categories.

Let me trace through what the complete flow would look like:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    OPCODE ACP CONFIG OPTIONS                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                          │
│   NewSessionResponse returns:                                            │
│   ├── sessionId: string                                                  │
│   ├── configOptions?: SessionConfigOption[]                              │
│   │   └── Contains model/mode selectors with categories                 │
│   ├── models?: SessionModelState (legacy/experimental)                   │
│   └── modes?: SessionModeState (legacy/experimental)                       │
│                                                                          │
│   SessionConfigOption with category "model" or "mode":                    │
│   ├── id: string (e.g., "model" or "mode")                                │
│   ├── name: string (e.g., "Model" or "Mode")                             │
│   ├── category: "model" | "mode" | "thought_level" | "other"             │
│   ├── type: "select" | "boolean"                                        │
│   └── For select type:                                                   │
│       ├── currentValue: string (selected option id)                      │
│       └── options: SessionConfigSelectOption[] | SessionConfigSelectGroup[]
│           └── { value, name, description? }                               │
│                                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

So the opencode ACP server returns models/modes as config options, not as the separate `models`/`modes` fields. This is actually the newer approach in the protocol.

Now I have a complete picture. Let me summarize what we've discovered:
## Agent (2026-04-04 10:42:25)

Excellent! Now I have the complete picture. Let me visualize what we've discovered:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    OPCODE ACP SERVER FLOW                                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                          │
│   When `opencode acp` creates a session:                                  │
│                                                                          │
│   NewSessionResponse {                                                   │
│     sessionId: "abc-123"                                                  │
│     configOptions: [                       ← MODELS/MODES ARE HERE       │
│       {                                                                   │
│         id: "model",                                                        │
│         name: "Model",                                                      │
│         category: "model",                                                  │
│         type: "select",                                                     │
│         currentValue: "kimik2.5",                                         │
│         options: [                                                          │
│           { value: "kimik2.5", name: "Kimi K2.5" },                       │
│           { value: "claude-3", name: "Claude 3" },                          │
│           { value: "gpt-4", name: "GPT-4" }                               │
│         ]                                                                   │
│       },                                                                  │
│       {                                                                   │
│         id: "mode",                                                         │
│         name: "Mode",                                                       │
│         category: "mode",                                                   │
│         type: "select",                                                     │
│         currentValue: "explore",                                            │
│         options: [                                                          │
│           { value: "explore", name: "Explore" },                          │
│           { value: "code", name: "Code" },                                │
│           { value: "review", name: "Review" }                             │
│         ]                                                                   │
│       }                                                                   │
│     ]                                                                     │
│     // Note: models/modes fields also exist but are deprecated/unstable   │
│   }                                                                       │
│                                                                          │
│   SessionConfigSelect structure:                                          │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│   │ SessionConfigOption                                               │ │
│   │ ├── id: "model" | "mode" | "thought_level"                        │ │
│   │ ├── name: display label                                           │ │
│   │ ├── category: semantic type                                       │ │
│   │ ├── type: "select"                                                │ │
│   │ └── (select payload):                                             │ │
│   │     ├── currentValue: selected option id                          │ │
│   │     └── options: flat or grouped list                             │ │
│   │         └── { value, name, description? }                           │ │
│   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key findings:**

1. **Models/modes are in `configOptions`**, not the separate `models`/`modes` fields (those are legacy/unstable)

2. **ConfigOption category system:**
   - `"model"` - Model selector
   - `"mode"` - Session mode selector  
   - `"thought_level"` - Reasoning level
   - `"other"` - Uncategorized

3. **Current mimo-agent** (lines 457-464 in index.ts) ignores `configOptions` entirely:
   ```typescript
   session.acpConnection!.newSession({
     cwd: session.checkoutPath,
     mcpServers: [],
   })
   // Response comes back with configOptions, but we only extract sessionId
   ```

**The updated implementation path:**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    UPDATED IMPLEMENTATION                                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                          │
│  MIMO-AGENT (mimo-agent/src/index.ts)                                    │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ After newSession():                                                  │ │
│  │                                                                      │ │
│  │ 1. Extract configOptions from response                              │ │
│  │ 2. Find option with category === "model"                            │ │
│  │    → currentValue = selected model id                               │ │
│  │    → options = available models list                                │ │
│  │ 3. Find option with category === "mode"                             │ │
│  │    → currentValue = selected mode id                                │ │
│  │    → options = available modes list                                 │ │
│  │                                                                      │ │
│  │ 4. Send to platform via WebSocket:                                   │ │
│  │    {                                                                 │ │
│  │      type: "session_initialized",                                   │ │
│  │      sessionId: "...",                                              │ │
│  │      modelState: { currentModelId, availableModels },               │ │
│  │      modeState: { currentModeId, availableModes }                    │ │
│  │    }                                                                │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                          │
│  PLATFORM (mimo-platform)                                                  │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ WebSocket handler receives session_initialized:                      │ │
│  │                                                                      │ │
│  │ 1. Store in memory (no DB persistence needed)                       │ │
│  │ 2. Broadcast to UI: { type: "model_state", ... }                   │ │
│  │ 3. Broadcast to UI: { type: "mode_state", ... }                      │ │
│  │                                                                      │ │
│  │ When UI sends change request:                                       │ │
│  │ 1. Forward to mimo-agent via WebSocket                              │ │
│  │ 2. Mimo-agent calls setSessionConfigOption on ACP                   │ │
│  │    (or unstable_setSessionModel if using legacy API)                │ │
│  │ 3. ACP may respond with updated configOptions                       │ │
│  │ 4. Broadcast update to UI                                           │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                          │
│  UI (SessionDetailPage.tsx + chat.js)                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ Header shows:                                                        │ │
│  │                                                                      │ │
│  │ Model: [Kimi K2.5 ▼]    Mode: [Explore ▼]                            │ │
│  │                                                                      │ │
│  │ Dropdowns populated from WebSocket state                             │ │
│  │ Selection sends: { type: "set_model", modelId }                      │ │
│  │                  { type: "set_mode", modeId }                       │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key questions answered:**

1. ✓ **Where do models come from?** - `configOptions` array, find by `category: "model"`
2. ✓ **Where do modes come from?** - `configOptions` array, find by `category: "mode"`
3. ✓ **Default selection?** - Use `currentValue` from the config option
4. ✓ **How to change?** - Use `setSessionConfigOption` or `unstable_setSessionModel`

**To set model/mode via ACP:**
- Via config options: `setSessionConfigOption({ sessionId, optionId: "model", value: "claude-3" })`
- Via legacy API: `unstable_setSessionModel({ sessionId, modelId: "claude-3" })`

The opencode server likely supports both. The config option approach is the "modern" way.

Ready to capture this into an OpenSpec change when you are!

<skill_content name="openspec-new-change">
# Skill: openspec-new-change

Start a new change using the experimental artifact-driven approach.

**Input**: The user's request should include a change name (kebab-case) OR a description of what they want to build.

**Steps**

1. **If no clear input provided, ask what they want to build**

   Use the **AskUserQuestion tool** (open-ended, no preset options) to ask:
   > "What change do you want to work on? Describe what you want to build or fix."

   From their description, derive a kebab-case name (e.g., "add user authentication" → `add-user-auth`).

   **IMPORTANT**: Do NOT proceed without understanding what the user wants to build.

2. **Determine the workflow schema**

   Use the default schema (omit `--schema`) unless the user explicitly requests a different workflow.

   **Use a different schema only if the user mentions:**
   - A specific schema name → use `--schema <name>`
   - "show workflows" or "what workflows" → run `openspec schemas --json` and let them choose

   **Otherwise**: Omit `--schema` to use the default.

3. **Create the change directory**
   ```bash
   openspec new change "<name>"
   ```
   Add `--schema <name>` only if the user requested a specific workflow.
   This creates a scaffolded change at `openspec/changes/<name>/` with the selected schema.

4. **Show the artifact status**
   ```bash
   openspec status --change "<name>"
   ```
   This shows which artifacts need to be created and which are ready (dependencies satisfied).

5. **Get instructions for the first artifact**
   The first artifact depends on the schema (e.g., `proposal` for spec-driven).
   Check the status output to find the first artifact with status "ready".
   ```bash
   openspec instructions <first-artifact-id> --change "<name>"
   ```
   This outputs the template and context for creating the first artifact.

6. **STOP and wait for user direction**

**Output**

After completing the steps, summarize:
- Change name and location
- Schema/workflow being used and its artifact sequence
- Current status (0/N artifacts complete)
- The template for the first artifact
- Prompt: "Ready to create the first artifact? Just describe what this change is about and I'll draft it, or ask me to continue."

**Guardrails**
- Do NOT create any artifacts yet - just show the instructions
- Do NOT advance beyond showing the first artifact template
- If the name is invalid (not kebab-case), ask for a valid name
- If a change with that name already exists, suggest continuing that change instead
- Pass --schema if using a non-default workflow

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/src/mimo/.opencode/skills/openspec-new-change
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

## Agent (2026-04-04 10:43:37)

Perfect! I've created the OpenSpec change `session-model-mode-selector`. 

**Status:** 0/4 artifacts complete  
**Schema:** spec-driven  
**Location:** `openspec/changes/session-model-mode-selector/`

## Proposal Template

Here's what we need to create for the proposal:

```markdown
## Why

Users need to manually select which LLM model and operational mode to use for each session. Currently, the mimo-agent doesn't expose the model/mode configuration options that the opencode ACP server provides via `configOptions`. This limits user control over their AI assistant's behavior and capabilities.

#### What Changes

- Add model and mode selector dropdowns to the Session Detail page UI
- Capture `configOptions` from ACP `NewSessionResponse` in mimo-agent
- Forward model/mode state from mimo-agent to mimo-platform via WebSocket
- Store model/mode state in-memory per-session (no persistence)
- Handle user-initiated model/mode changes and forward to ACP via `setSessionConfigOption`
- Support config option categories: `"model"` and `"mode"`

#### Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `session-model-selector`: UI component and WebSocket handling for selecting LLM models from available options
- `session-mode-selector`: UI component and WebSocket handling for selecting operational modes (explore, code, review, etc.)

##### Modified Capabilities
- None (this is purely additive)

#### Impact

- **mimo-platform**: SessionDetailPage.tsx UI changes, WebSocket handlers, in-memory session state
- **mimo-agent**: Capture and forward configOptions from ACP, handle setSessionConfigOption calls
- **Dependencies**: ACP SDK `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` for types and methods
- **Breaking Changes**: None
```

Ready to create the proposal? Just say yes, or tell me if you want to adjust anything first!

275:      * Optional description providing more details about this authentication method.
276:      */
277:     description?: string | null;
278:     /**
279:      * Unique identifier for this authentication method.
280:      */
281:     id: string;
282:     /**
283:      * Human-readable name of the authentication method.
284:      */
285:     name: string;
286: };
287: /**
288:  * **UNSTABLE**
289:  *
290:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
291:  *
292:  * Environment variable authentication method.
293:  *
294:  * The user provides credentials that the client passes to the agent as environment variables.
295:  *
296:  * @experimental
297:  */
298: export type AuthMethodEnvVar = {
299:     /**
300:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
301:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
302:      * these keys.
303:      *
304:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
305:      */
306:     _meta?: {
307:         [key: string]: unknown;
308:     } | null;
309:     /**
310:      * Optional description providing more details about this authentication method.
311:      */
312:     description?: string | null;
313:     /**
314:      * Unique identifier for this authentication method.
315:      */
316:     id: string;
317:     /**
318:      * Optional link to a page where the user can obtain their credentials.
319:      */
320:     link?: string | null;
321:     /**
322:      * Human-readable name of the authentication method.
323:      */
324:     name: string;
325:     /**
326:      * The environment variables the client should set.
327:      */
328:     vars: Array<AuthEnvVar>;
329: };
330: /**
331:  * **UNSTABLE**
332:  *
333:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
334:  *
335:  * Terminal-based authentication method.
336:  *
337:  * The client runs an interactive terminal for the user to authenticate via a TUI.
338:  *
339:  * @experimental
340:  */
341: export type AuthMethodTerminal = {
342:     /**
343:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
344:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
345:      * these keys.
346:      *
347:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
348:      */
349:     _meta?: {
350:         [key: string]: unknown;
351:     } | null;
352:     /**
353:      * Additional arguments to pass when running the agent binary for terminal auth.
354:      */
355:     args?: Array<string>;
356:     /**
357:      * Optional description providing more details about this authentication method.
358:      */
359:     description?: string | null;
360:     /**
361:      * Additional environment variables to set when running the agent binary for terminal auth.
362:      */
363:     env?: {
364:         [key: string]: string;
365:     };
366:     /**
367:      * Unique identifier for this authentication method.
368:      */
369:     id: string;
370:     /**
371:      * Human-readable name of the authentication method.
372:      */
373:     name: string;
374: };
375: /**
376:  * Request parameters for the authenticate method.
377:  *
378:  * Specifies which authentication method to use.
379:  */
380: export type AuthenticateRequest = {
381:     /**
382:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
383:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
384:      * these keys.
385:      *
386:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
387:      */
388:     _meta?: {
389:         [key: string]: unknown;
390:     } | null;
391:     /**
392:      * The ID of the authentication method to use.
393:      * Must be one of the methods advertised in the initialize response.
394:      */
395:     methodId: string;
396: };
397: /**
398:  * Response to the `authenticate` method.
399:  */
400: export type AuthenticateResponse = {
401:     /**
402:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
403:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
404:      * these keys.
405:      *
406:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
407:      */
408:     _meta?: {
409:         [key: string]: unknown;
410:     } | null;
411: };
412: /**
413:  * Information about a command.
414:  */
415: export type AvailableCommand = {
416:     /**
417:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
418:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
419:      * these keys.
420:      *
421:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
422:      */
423:     _meta?: {
424:         [key: string]: unknown;
425:     } | null;
426:     /**
427:      * Human-readable description of what the command does.
428:      */
429:     description: string;
430:     /**
431:      * Input for the command if required
432:      */
433:     input?: AvailableCommandInput | null;
434:     /**
435:      * Command name (e.g., `create_plan`, `research_codebase`).
436:      */
437:     name: string;
438: };
439: /**
440:  * unstructured
441:  *
442:  * All text that was typed after the command name is provided as input.
443:  */
444: export type AvailableCommandInput = UnstructuredCommandInput;
445: /**
446:  * Available commands are ready or have changed
447:  */
448: export type AvailableCommandsUpdate = {
449:     /**
450:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
451:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
452:      * these keys.
453:      *
454:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
455:      */
456:     _meta?: {
457:         [key: string]: unknown;
458:     } | null;
459:     /**
460:      * Commands the agent can execute
461:      */
462:     availableCommands: Array<AvailableCommand>;
463: };
464: /**
465:  * Binary resource contents.
466:  */
467: export type BlobResourceContents = {
468:     /**
469:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
470:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
471:      * these keys.
472:      *
473:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
474:      */
475:     _meta?: {
476:         [key: string]: unknown;
477:     } | null;
478:     blob: string;
479:     mimeType?: string | null;
480:     uri: string;
481: };
482: /**
483:  * Schema for boolean properties in an elicitation form.
484:  */
485: export type BooleanPropertySchema = {
486:     /**
487:      * Default value.
488:      */
489:     default?: boolean | null;
490:     /**
491:      * Human-readable description.
492:      */
493:     description?: string | null;
494:     /**
495:      * Optional title for the property.
496:      */
497:     title?: string | null;
498: };
499: /**
500:  * Notification to cancel ongoing operations for a session.
501:  *
502:  * See protocol docs: [Cancellation](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#cancellation)
503:  */
504: export type CancelNotification = {
505:     /**
506:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
507:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
508:      * these keys.
509:      *
510:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
511:      */
512:     _meta?: {
513:         [key: string]: unknown;
514:     } | null;
515:     /**
516:      * The ID of the session to cancel operations for.
517:      */
518:     sessionId: SessionId;
519: };
520: /**
521:  * **UNSTABLE**
522:  *
523:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
524:  *
525:  * Notification to cancel an ongoing request.
526:  *
527:  * See protocol docs: [Cancellation](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/cancellation)
528:  *
529:  * @experimental
530:  */
531: export type CancelRequestNotification = {
532:     /**
533:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
534:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
535:      * these keys.
536:      *
537:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
538:      */
539:     _meta?: {
540:         [key: string]: unknown;
541:     } | null;
542:     /**
543:      * The ID of the request to cancel.
544:      */
545:     requestId: RequestId;
546: };
547: /**
548:  * Capabilities supported by the client.
549:  *
550:  * Advertised during initialization to inform the agent about
551:  * available features and methods.
552:  *
553:  * See protocol docs: [Client Capabilities](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization#client-capabilities)
554:  */
555: export type ClientCapabilities = {
556:     /**
557:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
558:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
559:      * these keys.
560:      *
561:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
562:      */
563:     _meta?: {
564:         [key: string]: unknown;
565:     } | null;
566:     /**
567:      * **UNSTABLE**
568:      *
569:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
570:      *
571:      * Authentication capabilities supported by the client.
572:      * Determines which authentication method types the agent may include
573:      * in its `InitializeResponse`.
574:      *
575:      * @experimental
576:      */
577:     auth?: AuthCapabilities;
578:     /**
579:      * **UNSTABLE**
580:      *
581:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
582:      *
583:      * Elicitation capabilities supported by the client.
584:      * Determines which elicitation modes the agent may use.
585:      *
586:      * @experimental
587:      */
588:     elicitation?: ElicitationCapabilities | null;
589:     /**
590:      * File system capabilities supported by the client.
591:      * Determines which file operations the agent can request.
592:      */
593:     fs?: FileSystemCapabilities;
594:     /**
595:      * **UNSTABLE**
596:      *
597:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
598:      *
599:      * NES (Next Edit Suggestions) capabilities supported by the client.
600:      *
601:      * @experimental
602:      */
603:     nes?: ClientNesCapabilities | null;
604:     /**
605:      * **UNSTABLE**
606:      *
607:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
608:      *
609:      * The position encodings supported by the client, in order of preference.
610:      *
611:      * @experimental
612:      */
613:     positionEncodings?: Array<PositionEncodingKind>;
614:     /**
615:      * Whether the Client support all `terminal*` methods.
616:      */
617:     terminal?: boolean;
618: };
619: /**
620:  * NES capabilities advertised by the client during initialization.
621:  */
622: export type ClientNesCapabilities = {
623:     /**
624:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
625:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
626:      * these keys.
627:      *
628:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
629:      */
630:     _meta?: {
631:         [key: string]: unknown;
632:     } | null;
633:     /**
634:      * Whether the client supports the `jump` suggestion kind.
635:      */
636:     jump?: NesJumpCapabilities | null;
637:     /**
638:      * Whether the client supports the `rename` suggestion kind.
639:      */
640:     rename?: NesRenameCapabilities | null;
641:     /**
642:      * Whether the client supports the `searchAndReplace` suggestion kind.
643:      */
644:     searchAndReplace?: NesSearchAndReplaceCapabilities | null;
645: };
646: export type ClientNotification = {
647:     method: string;
648:     params?: CancelNotification | DidOpenDocumentNotification | DidChangeDocumentNotification | DidCloseDocumentNotification | DidSaveDocumentNotification | DidFocusDocumentNotification | AcceptNesNotification | RejectNesNotification | ExtNotification | null;
649: };
650: export type ClientRequest = {
651:     id: RequestId;
652:     method: string;
653:     params?: InitializeRequest | AuthenticateRequest | LogoutRequest | NewSessionRequest | LoadSessionRequest | ListSessionsRequest | ForkSessionRequest | ResumeSessionRequest | CloseSessionRequest | SetSessionModeRequest | SetSessionConfigOptionRequest | PromptRequest | SetSessionModelRequest | StartNesRequest | SuggestNesRequest | CloseNesRequest | ExtRequest | null;
654: };
655: export type ClientResponse = {
656:     id: RequestId;
657:     /**
658:      * All possible responses that a client can send to an agent.
659:      *
660:      * This enum is used internally for routing RPC responses. You typically won't need
661:      * to use this directly - the responses are handled automatically by the connection.
662:      *
663:      * These are responses to the corresponding `AgentRequest` variants.
664:      */
665:     result: WriteTextFileResponse | ReadTextFileResponse | RequestPermissionResponse | CreateTerminalResponse | TerminalOutputResponse | ReleaseTerminalResponse | WaitForTerminalExitResponse | KillTerminalResponse | ElicitationResponse | ExtResponse;
666: } | {
667:     error: Error;
668:     id: RequestId;
669: };
670: /**
671:  * Request to close an NES session.
672:  *
673:  * The agent **must** cancel any ongoing work related to the NES session
674:  * and then free up any resources associated with the session.
675:  */
676: export type CloseNesRequest = {
677:     /**
678:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
679:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
680:      * these keys.
681:      *
682:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
683:      */
684:     _meta?: {
685:         [key: string]: unknown;
686:     } | null;
687:     /**
688:      * The ID of the NES session to close.
689:      */
690:     sessionId: SessionId;
691: };
692: /**
693:  * Response from closing an NES session.
694:  */
695: export type CloseNesResponse = {
696:     /**
697:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
698:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
699:      * these keys.
700:      *
701:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
702:      */
703:     _meta?: {
704:         [key: string]: unknown;
705:     } | null;
706: };
707: /**
708:  * **UNSTABLE**
709:  *
710:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
711:  *
712:  * Request parameters for closing an active session.
713:  *
714:  * If supported, the agent **must** cancel any ongoing work related to the session
715:  * (treat it as if `session/cancel` was called) and then free up any resources
716:  * associated with the session.
717:  *
718:  * Only available if the Agent supports the `session.close` capability.
719:  *
720:  * @experimental
721:  */
722: export type CloseSessionRequest = {
723:     /**
724:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
725:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
726:      * these keys.
727:      *
728:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
729:      */
730:     _meta?: {
731:         [key: string]: unknown;
732:     } | null;
733:     /**
734:      * The ID of the session to close.
735:      */
736:     sessionId: SessionId;
737: };
738: /**
739:  * **UNSTABLE**
740:  *
741:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
742:  *
743:  * Response from closing a session.
744:  *
745:  * @experimental
746:  */
747: export type CloseSessionResponse = {
748:     /**
749:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
750:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
751:      * these keys.
752:      *
753:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
754:      */
755:     _meta?: {
756:         [key: string]: unknown;
757:     } | null;
758: };
759: /**
760:  * Session configuration options have been updated.
761:  */
762: export type ConfigOptionUpdate = {
763:     /**
764:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
765:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
766:      * these keys.
767:      *
768:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
769:      */
770:     _meta?: {
771:         [key: string]: unknown;
772:     } | null;
773:     /**
774:      * The full set of configuration options and their current values.
775:      */
776:     configOptions: Array<SessionConfigOption>;
777: };
778: /**
779:  * Standard content block (text, images, resources).
780:  */
781: export type Content = {
782:     /**
783:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
784:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
785:      * these keys.
786:      *
787:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
788:      */
789:     _meta?: {
790:         [key: string]: unknown;
791:     } | null;
792:     /**
793:      * The actual content block.
794:      */
795:     content: ContentBlock;
796: };
797: /**
798:  * Content blocks represent displayable information in the Agent Client Protocol.
799:  *
800:  * They provide a structured way to handle various types of user-facing content—whether
801:  * it's text from language models, images for analysis, or embedded resources for context.
802:  *
803:  * Content blocks appear in:
804:  * - User prompts sent via `session/prompt`
805:  * - Language model output streamed through `session/update` notifications
806:  * - Progress updates and results from tool calls
807:  *
808:  * This structure is compatible with the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling
809:  * agents to seamlessly forward content from MCP tool outputs without transformation.
810:  *
811:  * See protocol docs: [Content](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/content)
812:  */
813: export type ContentBlock = (TextContent & {
814:     type: "text";
815: }) | (ImageContent & {
816:     type: "image";
817: }) | (AudioContent & {
818:     type: "audio";
819: }) | (ResourceLink & {
820:     type: "resource_link";
821: }) | (EmbeddedResource & {
822:     type: "resource";
823: });
824: /**
825:  * A streamed item of content
826:  */
827: export type ContentChunk = {
828:     /**
829:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
830:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
831:      * these keys.
832:      *
833:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
834:      */
835:     _meta?: {
836:         [key: string]: unknown;
837:     } | null;
838:     /**
839:      * A single item of content
840:      */
841:     content: ContentBlock;
842:     /**
843:      * **UNSTABLE**
844:      *
845:      * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
846:      *
847:      * A unique identifier for the message this chunk belongs to.
848:      *
849:      * All chunks belonging to the same message share the same `messageId`.
850:      * A change in `messageId` indicates a new message has started.
851:      * Both clients and agents MUST use UUID format for message IDs.
852:      *
853:      * @experimental
854:      */
855:     messageId?: string | null;
856: };
857: /**
858:  * **UNSTABLE**
859:  *
860:  * This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.
861:  *
862:  * Cost information for a session.
863:  *
864:  * @experimental
865:  */
866: export type Cost = {
867:     /**
868:      * Total cumulative cost for session.
869:      */
870:     amount: number;
871:     /**
872:      * ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., "USD", "EUR").
873:      */
874:     currency: string;
875: };
876: /**
877:  * Request to create a new terminal and execute a command.
878:  */
879: export type CreateTerminalRequest = {
880:     /**
881:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
882:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
883:      * these keys.
884:      *
885:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
886:      */
887:     _meta?: {
888:         [key: string]: unknown;
889:     } | null;
890:     /**
891:      * Array of command arguments.
892:      */
893:     args?: Array<string>;
894:     /**
895:      * The command to execute.
896:      */
897:     command: string;
898:     /**
899:      * Working directory for the command (absolute path).
900:      */
901:     cwd?: string | null;
902:     /**
903:      * Environment variables for the command.
904:      */
905:     env?: Array<EnvVariable>;
906:     /**
907:      * Maximum number of output bytes to retain.
908:      *
909:      * When the limit is exceeded, the Client truncates from the beginning of the output
910:      * to stay within the limit.
911:      *
912:      * The Client MUST ensure truncation happens at a character boundary to maintain valid
913:      * string output, even if this means the retained output is slightly less than the
914:      * specified limit.
915:      */
916:     outputByteLimit?: number | null;
917:     /**
918:      * The session ID for this request.
919:      */
920:     sessionId: SessionId;
921: };
922: /**
923:  * Response containing the ID of the created terminal.
924:  */
925: export type CreateTerminalResponse = {
926:     /**
927:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
928:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
929:      * these keys.
930:      *
931:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
932:      */
933:     _meta?: {
934:         [key: string]: unknown;
935:     } | null;
936:     /**
937:      * The unique identifier for the created terminal.
938:      */
939:     terminalId: string;
940: };
941: /**
942:  * The current mode of the session has changed
943:  *
944:  * See protocol docs: [Session Modes](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-modes)
945:  */
946: export type CurrentModeUpdate = {
947:     /**
948:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
949:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
950:      * these keys.
951:      *
952:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
953:      */
954:     _meta?: {
955:         [key: string]: unknown;
956:     } | null;
957:     /**
958:      * The ID of the current mode
959:      */
960:     currentModeId: SessionModeId;
961: };
962: /**
963:  * Notification sent when a file is edited.
964:  */
965: export type DidChangeDocumentNotification = {
966:     /**
967:      * The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
968:      * metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
969:      * these keys.
970:      *
971:      * See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)
972:      */
973:     _meta?: {
974:         [key: string]: unknown;
975:     } | null;
976:     /**
977:      * The content changes.
978:      */
979:     contentChanges: Array<TextDocumentContentChangeEvent>;
980:     /**
981:      * The session ID for this notification.
982:      */
983:     sessionId: SessionId;
984:     /**
985:      * The URI of the changed document.
986:      */
987:     uri: string;
988:     /**
989:      * The new version number of the document.
990:      */
991:     version: number;
992: };
993: /**
994:  * Notification sent when a file is closed.
995:  */
996: export type DidCloseDocumentNotification = {
997:     /**
