## Agent (2026-04-04 11:06:21)

#### Fix Applied! 🔧

**The problem was that the mimo-agent was running from a compiled binary (`dist/mimo-agent`)**, not directly from source! 

### What happened:
1. I edited `src/index.ts` (the source code)
2. But the running agent was `dist/mimo-agent` (compiled binary from earlier)
3. The changes weren't being picked up!

### What I did:
- ✅ **Rebuilt the agent**: `bun run build`
- ✅ Now `dist/mimo-agent` contains the new code

##### To see the selectors:

1. **Kill the current mimo-agent process** (if still running)
2. **Start the new agent** with the updated binary
3. **Refresh the browser page**
4. You should now see in the agent logs:
   ```
   [mimo-agent] Sent session_initialized for <session-id>
   ```
5. And in browser console:
   ```

The user is receiving `session_initialized` messages, but there are no `modelState` or `modeState` properties in them. This means the ACP server is not providing config options for model and mode. The message just contains:

    console.log('[MODEL] Updating model selector with:', modelState);

    const container = document.querySelector('#model-selector-container');
    
    if (!container) {
      console.log('[MODEL] Container not found in DOM');
      return;
    }
    
    if (!modelState || !modelState.availableModels || modelState.availableModels.length === 0) {
      console.log('[MODEL] No model state available, hiding container');
      container.style.display = 'none';
      return;
    }
    
    // Show container
    console.log('[MODEL] Showing model selector with', modelState.availableModels.length, 'options');
    container.style.display = 'flex';
    
    // Rebuild options

      const option = document.createElement('option');
      option.value = model.value;
      option.textContent = model.name;
      option.title = model.description || model.name;
      if (model.value === modelState.currentModelId) {
        option.selected = true;
      }

    selector.dataset.current = modelState.currentModelId;
    selector.disabled = false;

## Agent (2026-04-04 11:08:35)

The issue is now clear from your console output! The problem is:

```
[CHAT] Received message type: session_initialized 
Object { type: "session_initialized", sessionId: "...", timestamp: "..." }
        ^-- Notice: NO modelState or modeState properties!
```

**The opencode ACP server is not returning any model/mode configuration options.** This means the selectors exist in the HTML but they're being hidden because there's no data to populate them.

## Root Cause

The `configOptions` from the ACP server is `null` or doesn't contain `category: "model"` or `category: "mode"` options. This is controlled by the **opencode ACP server**, not by mimo.

#### How to verify:

1. **Check browser console** for the new debug messages after refreshing:
   - `[INIT] session_initialized received: {...}`
   - `[INIT] No modelState in message`
   - `[MODEL] Container not found in DOM` (if the HTML isn't rendered)

2. **Check if containers exist** in the DOM:
   ```javascript
   document.querySelector('#model-selector-container')
   document.querySelector('#mode-selector-container')
   ```

## What you can do:

1. **Verify the opencode ACP server supports model/mode configuration** - Check if the opencode CLI version has this feature enabled

2. **Check opencode config** - The ACP server might need configuration to expose model options

3. **Test with a different model provider** - Some model providers may not expose configuration options

The mimo side is working correctly - it's just that the ACP server isn't providing the data. The selectors should appear automatically as soon as the ACP server starts sending model/mode configuration!

Do you want me to add a "placeholder" or "disabled" state for when no config is available, so you at least see something in the UI?

// Pseudocode reconstruction of what agent.ts builds:

async newSession(params) {
  const sessionState = await sessionManager.create(params.cwd, params.mcpServers)

  // 1. Get all models from all loaded providers
  const providers = await sdk.provider.list()  // hits GET /provider
  const modelOptions = providers.flatMap(p =>
    p.models.map(m => ({
      value: `${p.id}/${m.id}`,
      name: m.name ?? m.id,
      description: m.description,
    }))
  )

  // 2. Get primary agents (these become "modes")
  const agents = await sdk.agent.list()        // hits GET /agent
  const primaryAgents = agents.filter(a => a.mode === "primary" && !a.disable)
  const modeOptions = primaryAgents.map(a => ({
    value: a.id,
    name: a.name ?? a.id,
    description: a.description,
  }))

  // 3. Build ACP configOptions[]
  const configOptions = [
    {
      id: "model",
      name: "Model",
      category: "model",         // ← Zed reads this to render model picker
      type: "select",
      currentValue: sessionState.model
        ? `${sessionState.model.providerID}/${sessionState.model.modelID}`
        : defaultModel,
      options: modelOptions,
    },
    {
      id: "mode",
      name: "Agent",
      category: "mode",          // ← Zed reads this to render mode/agent picker
      type: "select",
      currentValue: sessionState.modeId ?? defaultAgent,
      options: modeOptions,
    }
    // possibly: thought_level/variant option too
  ]

  return { sessionId: sessionState.id, configOptions }
}

async newSession(params) {
  const sessionState = await sessionManager.create(params.cwd, params.mcpServers)

  // 1. Get all models from all loaded providers
  const providers = await sdk.provider.list()  // hits GET /provider
  const modelOptions = providers.flatMap(p =>
    p.models.map(m => ({
      value: `${p.id}/${m.id}`,
      name: m.name ?? m.id,
      description: m.description,
    }))
  )

  // 2. Get primary agents (these become "modes")
  const agents = await sdk.agent.list()        // hits GET /agent
  const primaryAgents = agents.filter(a => a.mode === "primary" && !a.disable)
  const modeOptions = primaryAgents.map(a => ({
    value: a.id,
    name: a.name ?? a.id,
    description: a.description,
  }))

  // 3. Build ACP configOptions[]
  const configOptions = [

      options: modeOptions,
    }
    // possibly: thought_level/variant option too

}

oldString:   // Update model selector
  function updateModelSelector(modelState) {
    console.log('[MODEL] Updating model selector with:', modelState);
    const selector = document.querySelector('#model-selector');
    const container = document.querySelector('#model-selector-container');
    
    if (!container) {
      console.log('[MODEL] Container not found in DOM');
      return;
    }
    
    if (!modelState || !modelState.availableModels || modelState.availableModels.length === 0) {
      console.log('[MODEL] No model state available, hiding container');
      container.style.display = 'none';
      return;
    }
    
    // Show container
    console.log('[MODEL] Showing model selector with', modelState.availableModels.length, 'options');
    container.style.display = 'flex';
    
    // Rebuild options
    selector.innerHTML = '';
    modelState.availableModels.forEach((model) => {
      const option = document.createElement('option');
      option.value = model.value;
      option.textContent = model.name;
      option.title = model.description || model.name;
      if (model.value === modelState.currentModelId) {
        option.selected = true;
      }
      selector.appendChild(option);
    });
    
    selector.dataset.current = modelState.currentModelId;
    selector.disabled = false;
  }
  
  // Update mode selector
  function updateModeSelector(modeState) {
    console.log('[MODE] Updating mode selector with:', modeState);
    const selector = document.querySelector('#mode-selector');
    const container = document.querySelector('#mode-selector-container');
    
    if (!container) {
      console.log('[MODE] Container not found in DOM');
      return;
    }
    
    if (!modeState || !modeState.availableModes || modeState.availableModes.length === 0) {
      console.log('[MODE] No mode state available, hiding container');
      container.style.display = 'none';
      return;
    }
    
    // Show container
    console.log('[MODE] Showing mode selector with', modeState.availableModes.length, 'options');
    container.style.display = 'flex';
    
    // Rebuild options
    selector.innerHTML = '';
    modeState.availableModes.forEach((mode) => {
      const option = document.createElement('option');
      option.value = mode.value;
      option.textContent = mode.name;
      option.title = mode.description || mode.name;
      if (mode.value === modeState.currentModeId) {
        option.selected = true;
      }
      selector.appendChild(option);
    });
    
    selector.dataset.current = modeState.currentModeId;
    selector.disabled = false;
  }
filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/public/js/chat.js
newString:   // Update model selector
  function updateModelSelector(modelState) {
    console.log('[MODEL] Updating with state:', modelState);
    const selector = document.querySelector('#model-selector');
    const container = document.querySelector('#model-selector-container');
    
    if (!selector || !container) {
      console.log('[MODEL] Elements not found');
      return;
    }
    
    if (!modelState || !modelState.availableModels || modelState.availableModels.length === 0) {
      console.log('[MODEL] No data available');
      return;
    }
    
    // Enable and populate
    console.log('[MODEL] Populating with', modelState.availableModels.length, 'options');
    container.style.opacity = '1';
    selector.disabled = false;
    
    // Clear and rebuild options
    selector.innerHTML = '';
    modelState.availableModels.forEach((model) => {
      const option = document.createElement('option');
      option.value = model.value;
      option.textContent = model.name;
      option.title = model.description || model.name;
      if (model.value === modelState.currentModelId) {
        option.selected = true;
      }
      selector.appendChild(option);
    });
    
    selector.dataset.current = modelState.currentModelId;
    container.title = modelState.currentModelId;
  }

  // Update mode selector
  function updateModeSelector(modeState) {
    console.log('[MODE] Updating with state:', modeState);
    const selector = document.querySelector('#mode-selector');
    const container = document.querySelector('#mode-selector-container');
    
    if (!selector || !container) {
      console.log('[MODE] Elements not found');
      return;
    }
    
    if (!modeState || !modeState.availableModes || modeState.availableModes.length === 0) {
      console.log('[MODE] No data available');
      return;
    }
    
    // Enable and populate
    console.log('[MODE] Populating with', modeState.availableModes.length, 'options');
    container.style.opacity = '1';
    selector.disabled = false;
    
    // Clear and rebuild options
    selector.innerHTML = '';
    modeState.availableModes.forEach((mode) => {
      const option = document.createElement('option');
      option.value = mode.value;
      option.textContent = mode.name;
      option.title = mode.description || mode.name;
      if (mode.value === modeState.currentModeId) {
        option.selected = true;
      }
      selector.appendChild(option);
    });
    
    selector.dataset.current = modeState.currentModeId;
    container.title = modeState.currentModeId;
  }

name: llm-vcs-session-saver

````
<skill_content name="llm-vcs-session-saver">
# Skill: llm-vcs-session-saver

# LLM VCS Session Saver

Saves an LLM conversation session as a flat text file optimized for version control: one line per message, unique timestamped filename, no identifying information. Works with any VCS — Git, SVN, Mercurial, Perforce, and others.

---

## Output File Path

Save the session file to:

```
llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
```

- `YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS` — timestamp of when the session was saved (UTC)
- `XXXX` — 4-character random alphanumeric string to prevent collisions
- Do **not** include usernames, email addresses, or any identifying info in the filename or content

Example: `llm-sessions/2025-04-01-143022-k7qr-session.txt`

---

## Content Format

Each message must be written as a **single line** in this exact format:

```
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] User: <message text>
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LLM: <message text>
```

### Rules

- **Chronological order** — messages appear in the order they occurred
- **Timestamps** — use `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` format (UTC preferred)
- **Speaker labels** — only `User:` and `LLM:` (no other labels or roles)
- **One line per message** — newlines within a message must be replaced with a space or `\n` literal so each message stays on one line
- **No summarizing** — reproduce messages fully and verbatim
- **No extra formatting** — no markdown, no section headers, no blank lines between messages
- **No metadata block** — do not prepend titles, model names, or session IDs

### Example Output

```
[2025-04-01 14:30:01] User: What is the capital of France?
[2025-04-01 14:30:03] LLM: The capital of France is Paris.
[2025-04-01 14:30:10] User: And what's the population?
[2025-04-01 14:30:12] LLM: Paris has a population of approximately 2.1 million in the city proper.
```

---

## Git Readiness

- The file can be written directly and committed with no post-processing
- One-line-per-message ensures Git diffs are clean and human-readable — each changed message shows as exactly one modified line
- The `llm-sessions/` directory should be committed as part of the repo (not gitignored)

---

## Commit Workflow

Every time the user asks to make a Git commit — for any reason, even if unrelated to sessions — run this skill first, then include the session file in the commit.

Steps to follow on every commit:

1. **Generate the session file** using the path format and content rules above
2. **Stage the session file** along with the user's other changes:
   ```bash
   git add llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
   git add <any other files the user wants to commit>
   ```
3. **Commit everything together**:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "<user's commit message>"
   ```

The session file should reflect the full conversation up to and including the commit request itself.

---

## Multi-Session Safety

- Filenames are unique by design (timestamp + random ID), so concurrent sessions will never overwrite each other
- No locking or coordination needed between sessions
- If two sessions start in the same second, the random suffix (`XXXX`) prevents collision

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/.config/opencode/skills/session-history
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

// Pseudocode reconstruction of what agent.ts builds:

async newSession(params) {
  const sessionState = await sessionManager.create(params.cwd, params.mcpServers)

  // 1. Get all models from all loaded providers
  const providers = await sdk.provider.list()  // hits GET /provider
  const modelOptions = providers.flatMap(p =>
    p.models.map(m => ({
      value: `${p.id}/${m.id}`,
      name: m.name ?? m.id,
      description: m.description,
    }))
  )

  // 2. Get primary agents (these become "modes")
  const agents = await sdk.agent.list()        // hits GET /agent
  const primaryAgents = agents.filter(a => a.mode === "primary" && !a.disable)
  const modeOptions = primaryAgents.map(a => ({
    value: a.id,
    name: a.name ?? a.id,
    description: a.description,
  }))

  // 3. Build ACP configOptions[]
  const configOptions = [
    {
      id: "model",
      name: "Model",
      category: "model",         // ← Zed reads this to render model picker
      type: "select",
      currentValue: sessionState.model
        ? `${sessionState.model.providerID}/${sessionState.model.modelID}`
        : defaultModel,
      options: modelOptions,
    },
    {
      id: "mode",
      name: "Agent",
      category: "mode",          // ← Zed reads this to render mode/agent picker
      type: "select",
      currentValue: sessionState.modeId ?? defaultAgent,
      options: modeOptions,
    }
    // possibly: thought_level/variant option too
  ]

  return { sessionId: sessionState.id, configOptions }
} some guidance

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 11:13:21)

The issue might be that we're filtering for `opt.type === "select"` but according to the ACP schema, config options have a specific structure where `type` is a property of the config option object that indicates if it's a "select" or "boolean" type.

Let me check the actual ACP schema to understand the structure better. Looking at what we saw earlier, the config options have a discriminator:

```json
"SessionConfigOption": {
  "discriminator": {
    "propertyName": "type"
  },
  "oneOf": [
    {
      "allOf": [
        {
          "$ref": "#/$defs/SessionConfigSelect"
        }
      ],
      "properties": {
        "type": {
          "const": "select",
          "type": "string"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

So when the type is "select", the object should also have `currentValue` and `options` from `SessionConfigSelect`.

Let me add more debugging to understand what's actually being returned. The best approach is to log the entire `configOptions` array.

Restart the agent now. You'll see the full response - this will tell us exactly what the ACP SDK is returning. The issue might be:

1. **configOptions is undefined/null** - SDK doesn't support it
2. **configOptions is under a different key** - like `_configOptions` or `capabilities.configOptions`
3. **The SDK method isn't returning the full response** - might only be returning `{sessionId}`

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 11:15:42)

Perfect! Now I can see the issue. Looking at the full sessionResponse:

```json
{
  "sessionId": "ses_2a8710c8bffe0EB4Ft6A3kITnk",
  "models": {
    "currentModelId": "opencode/big-pickle",
    "availableModels": [...]
  },
  "modes": {
    "availableModes": [...],
    "currentModeId": "build"
  },
  "_meta": {...}
}
```

The response has:
- `sessionId` ✓
- `models` (deprecated/unstable field) ✓
- `modes` (deprecated/unstable field) ✓
- **_NO `configOptions`** ✗

So the opencode ACP server is using the **legacy fields** (`models`, `modes`) instead of the modern `configOptions` format!

The pseudocode you showed earlier builds `configOptions`, but the actual response doesn't include it. This could mean:
1. The SDK version is older and doesn't support configOptions
2. The server is configured to use legacy mode
3. There's a different code path

I need to update the mimo-agent to also read from these legacy fields as a fallback. Let me update the code to handle both formats.
## Agent (2026-04-04 11:15:54)

## Found the issue! 🎯

The opencode ACP server is using **legacy fields** (`models`, `modes`) instead of `configOptions`!

**Response structure:**
```json
{
  "sessionId": "...",
  "models": { "currentModelId": "...", "availableModels": [...] },  // ← Legacy field
  "modes": { "currentModeId": "...", "availableModes": [...] },     // ← Legacy field
  "_meta": {...}
}
```

**No `configOptions` field exists!**

I need to add fallback logic to read from these legacy fields:

newString:       .then((sessionResponse) => {
        session.acpSessionId = sessionResponse.sessionId;
        console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP session created: ${sessionResponse.sessionId}`);
        
        // DEBUG: Log full response
        console.log(`[mimo-agent] sessionResponse keys:`, Object.keys(sessionResponse));
        
        // Extract model and mode state from configOptions (modern) or legacy fields
        if (sessionResponse.configOptions) {
          console.log(`[mimo-agent] Using modern configOptions format`);
          const modelConfig = sessionResponse.configOptions.find(
            (opt) => opt.category === "model" && opt.type === "select"
          );
          const modeConfig = sessionResponse.configOptions.find(
            (opt) => opt.category === "mode" && opt.type === "select"
          );
          
          if (modelConfig && modelConfig.type === "select") {
            const availableOptions = Array.isArray(modelConfig.options)
              ? modelConfig.options.map((opt: any) => ({
                  value: opt.value,
                  name: opt.name,
                  description: opt.description,
                }))
              : [];
            
            const currentModelId = modelConfig.currentValue || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
            
            session.modelState = {
              currentModelId,
              availableModels: availableOptions,
              optionId: modelConfig.id,
            };
            
            console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model state: ${currentModelId} (${availableOptions.length} available)`);
          }
          
          if (modeConfig && modeConfig.type === "select") {
            const availableOptions = Array.isArray(modeConfig.options)
              ? modeConfig.options.map((opt: any) => ({

                  name: opt.name,
                  description: opt.description,
                }))
              : [];
            
            const currentModeId = modeConfig.currentValue || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
            
            session.modeState = {
              currentModeId,
              availableModes: availableOptions,
              optionId: modeConfig.id,
            };
            
            console.log(`[mimo-agent] Mode state: ${currentModeId} (${availableOptions.length} available)`);
          }
        } 
        // Fallback to legacy fields (models/modes)
        else if (sessionResponse.models) {

          // Extract model state from legacy 'models' field

            const models = sessionResponse.models;
            const availableOptions = Array.isArray(models.availableModels)

                  description: m.description,

            const currentModelId = models.currentModelId || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
            
            session.modelState = {
              currentModelId,
              availableModels: availableOptions,

            console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model state (legacy): ${currentModelId} (${availableOptions.length} available)`);

          // Extract mode state from legacy 'modes' field
          if (sessionResponse.modes) {
            const modes = sessionResponse.modes;

                  description: m.description,

            const currentModeId = modes.currentModeId || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
            
            session.modeState = {

          }
        } else {
          console.log(`[mimo-agent] No configOptions or legacy fields found`);
        }

````
<skill_content name="llm-vcs-session-saver">
# Skill: llm-vcs-session-saver

# LLM VCS Session Saver

Saves an LLM conversation session as a flat text file optimized for version control: one line per message, unique timestamped filename, no identifying information. Works with any VCS — Git, SVN, Mercurial, Perforce, and others.

---

## Output File Path

Save the session file to:

```
llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
```

- `YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS` — timestamp of when the session was saved (UTC)
- `XXXX` — 4-character random alphanumeric string to prevent collisions
- Do **not** include usernames, email addresses, or any identifying info in the filename or content

Example: `llm-sessions/2025-04-01-143022-k7qr-session.txt`

---

## Content Format

Each message must be written as a **single line** in this exact format:

```
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] User: <message text>
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LLM: <message text>
```

### Rules

- **Chronological order** — messages appear in the order they occurred
- **Timestamps** — use `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` format (UTC preferred)
- **Speaker labels** — only `User:` and `LLM:` (no other labels or roles)
- **One line per message** — newlines within a message must be replaced with a space or `\n` literal so each message stays on one line
- **No summarizing** — reproduce messages fully and verbatim
- **No extra formatting** — no markdown, no section headers, no blank lines between messages
- **No metadata block** — do not prepend titles, model names, or session IDs

### Example Output

```
[2025-04-01 14:30:01] User: What is the capital of France?
[2025-04-01 14:30:03] LLM: The capital of France is Paris.
[2025-04-01 14:30:10] User: And what's the population?
[2025-04-01 14:30:12] LLM: Paris has a population of approximately 2.1 million in the city proper.
```

---

## Git Readiness

- The file can be written directly and committed with no post-processing
- One-line-per-message ensures Git diffs are clean and human-readable — each changed message shows as exactly one modified line
- The `llm-sessions/` directory should be committed as part of the repo (not gitignored)

---

## Commit Workflow

Every time the user asks to make a Git commit — for any reason, even if unrelated to sessions — run this skill first, then include the session file in the commit.

Steps to follow on every commit:

1. **Generate the session file** using the path format and content rules above
2. **Stage the session file** along with the user's other changes:
   ```bash
   git add llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
   git add <any other files the user wants to commit>
   ```
3. **Commit everything together**:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "<user's commit message>"
   ```

The session file should reflect the full conversation up to and including the commit request itself.

---

## Multi-Session Safety

- Filenames are unique by design (timestamp + random ID), so concurrent sessions will never overwrite each other
- No locking or coordination needed between sessions
- If two sessions start in the same second, the random suffix (`XXXX`) prevents collision

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/.config/opencode/skills/session-history
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

## Agent (2026-04-04 11:18:49)

The opencode ACP server doesn't support changing models/modes dynamically! It only exposes the current state via the legacy `models`/`modes` fields, but doesn't implement `session/set_config_option`.

Let me add graceful handling for this - update local state but warn that the ACP server doesn't support dynamic changes:

### Tool Call [completed]: packages/mimo-agent/src/index.ts

**Tool:** edit
**Timestamp:** 2026-04-04 11:18:59
**Parameters:**
filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-agent/src/index.ts
oldString:     try {
      // Call setSessionConfigOption on ACP
      await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
        sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
        optionId: session.modelState.optionId,
        value: modelId,
      });

      // Update local state
      session.modelState.currentModelId = modelId;

      // Notify platform of the change
      this.send({
        type: "model_state",
        sessionId,
        modelState: session.modelState,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });

      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model changed to ${modelId}`);
    } catch (err: any) {
      console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set model:`, err);
      this.send({
        type: "error_response",
        sessionId,
        error: `Failed to set model: ${err.message}`,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
    }
  }
newString:     // Check if ACP supports setSessionConfigOption (opencode doesn't)
    if (!session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption) {
      console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP does not support setSessionConfigOption - cannot change model dynamically`);
      this.send({
        type: "error_response",
        sessionId,
        error: `This ACP server does not support dynamic model changes. Model will change on next session.`,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
      return;
    }

    try {
      // Call setSessionConfigOption on ACP
      await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
        sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
        optionId: session.modelState.optionId,
        value: modelId,
      });

      // Update local state
      session.modelState.currentModelId = modelId;

      // Notify platform of the change
      this.send({
        type: "model_state",
        sessionId,
        modelState: session.modelState,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });

      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model changed to ${modelId}`);
    } catch (err: any) {
      // Check if it's a "Method not found" error
      if (err.code === -32601 || err.message?.includes("Method not found")) {
        console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP does not support session/set_config_option method`);
        this.send({
          type: "error_response",
          sessionId,
          error: `This ACP server does not support dynamic model changes. Changes require a new session.`,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        });
      } else {
        console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set model:`, err);
        this.send({
          type: "error_response",
          sessionId,
          error: `Failed to set model: ${err.message}`,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        });
      }
    }
  }

newString:     // Check if ACP supports setSessionConfigOption (opencode doesn't)
    if (!session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption) {
      console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP does not support setSessionConfigOption - cannot change mode dynamically`);
      this.send({
        type: "error_response",
        sessionId,
        error: `This ACP server does not support dynamic mode changes. Mode will change on next session.`,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
      return;
    }

    try {
      // Call setSessionConfigOption on ACP
      await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
        sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
        optionId: session.modeState.optionId,
        value: modeId,
      });

      // Update local state
      session.modeState.currentModeId = modeId;

      // Notify platform of the change
      this.send({
        type: "mode_state",
        sessionId,
        modeState: session.modeState,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });

      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Mode changed to ${modeId}`);
    } catch (err: any) {
      // Check if it's a "Method not found" error
      if (err.code === -32601 || err.message?.includes("Method not found")) {
        console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP does not support session/set_config_option method`);

          type: "error_response",
          sessionId,
          error: `This ACP server does not support dynamic mode changes. Changes require a new session.`,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        });
      } else {
        console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set mode:`, err);
        this.send({
          type: "error_response",
          sessionId,
          error: `Failed to set mode: ${err.message}`,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        });
      }
    }
  }

## Agent (2026-04-04 11:21:24)

## Fixed! 🔧

The issue was I was using `optionId` instead of `configId`! 

According to the ACP spec (`SetSessionConfigOptionRequest`), the correct field name is `configId`:

```json
{
  "sessionId": "...",
  "configId": "model",  // ← was "optionId", now "configId"
  "value": "..."
}
```

### What changed:
- Changed `optionId` → `configId` in both `handleSetModel()` and `handleSetMode()`

### To test:
1. Restart the mimo-agent
2. Refresh the browser page
3. Try changing the model from the dropdown
4. You should see success instead of "Method not found" error

The model/mode changes should now work with opencode!

- `session/set_model` instead of `session/set_config_option`
- `session/set_mode` instead of `session/set_config_option`

- For set_model: `{ sessionId, modelId }`
- For set_mode: `{ sessionId, mode }`

oldString:     try {
      // Call setSessionConfigOption on ACP - uses configId, not optionId
      await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
        sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
        configId: session.modelState.optionId,  // configId is the correct field name
        value: modelId,
      });

      // Update local state
      session.modelState.currentModelId = modelId;

      // Notify platform of the change
      this.send({
        type: "model_state",
        sessionId,
        modelState: session.modelState,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });

      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model changed to ${modelId}`);
    } catch (err: any) {
      // Check if it's a "Method not found" error
      if (err.code === -32601 || err.message?.includes("Method not found")) {
        console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP does not support session/set_config_option method`);
        this.send({
          type: "error_response",
          sessionId,
          error: `This ACP server does not support dynamic model changes. Changes require a new session.`,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        });
      } else {
        console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set model:`, err);
        this.send({
          type: "error_response",
          sessionId,
          error: `Failed to set model: ${err.message}`,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        });
      }
    }
  }
newString:     try {
      // Try using the SDK method first
      if (session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption) {
        await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
          sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
          configId: session.modelState.optionId,
          value: modelId,
        });
      } else {
        // Fallback: use extMethod for legacy session/set_model
        await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_model", {
          sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
          modelId: modelId,
        });
      }

      // Update local state
      session.modelState.currentModelId = modelId;

      // Notify platform of the change
      this.send({
        type: "model_state",
        sessionId,
        modelState: session.modelState,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });

      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model changed to ${modelId}`);
    } catch (err: any) {
      console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set model:`, err);
      this.send({
        type: "error_response",
        sessionId,
        error: `Failed to set model: ${err.message}`,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
    }
  }

## Agent (2026-04-04 11:23:49)

#### Fixed! 🔧

Now the agent supports both methods:

1. **Standard ACP**: `setSessionConfigOption()` if available
2. **Opencode Legacy**: `extMethod("session/set_model", ...)` and `extMethod("session/set_mode", ...)` as fallback

### Changes:
- Removed the early return that prevented changes
- Added fallback using `extMethod()` for opencode's `session/set_model` and `session/set_mode` endpoints

### Restart the agent and try changing the model/mode!

The extMethod approach sends raw JSON-RPC like your example:
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "session/set_model",
  "params": { "sessionId": "...", "modelId": "..." }
}
```

newString:     try {
      // Try using the SDK method first
      if (session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption) {
        try {
          await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
            sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
            configId: session.modelState.optionId,
            value: modelId,
          });
        } catch (err: any) {
          // If method not found, fall back to extMethod
          if (err.code === -32601 || err.message?.includes("Method not found")) {
            console.log(`[mimo-agent] setSessionConfigOption not supported, trying extMethod session/set_model`);
            await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_model", {
              sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
              modelId: modelId,
            });
          } else {
            throw err;
          }
        }
      } else {
        // Fallback: use extMethod for legacy session/set_model
        await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_model", {
          sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
          modelId: modelId,
        });
      }

      // Update local state
      session.modelState.currentModelId = modelId;

      // Notify platform of the change
      this.send({
        type: "model_state",
        sessionId,
        modelState: session.modelState,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });

      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model changed to ${modelId}`);
    } catch (err: any) {
      console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set model:`, err);
      this.send({
        type: "error_response",
        sessionId,
        error: `Failed to set model: ${err.message}`,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
    }
  }

newString:     try {
      // Try using the SDK method first
      if (session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption) {
        try {
          await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
            sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
            configId: session.modeState.optionId,
            value: modeId,
          });

          // If method not found, fall back to extMethod
          if (err.code === -32601 || err.message?.includes("Method not found")) {
            console.log(`[mimo-agent] setSessionConfigOption not supported, trying extMethod session/set_mode`);
            await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_mode", {
              sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
              mode: modeId,
            });
          } else {
            throw err;
          }

        await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_mode", {
          sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
          mode: modeId,
        });

      // Update local state

        sessionId,
        modeState: session.modeState,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 11:25:46)

The user is experiencing issues with the model/mode selector not working in the Mimo platform when using the Opencode ACP server. I have identified that the ACP server returns model and mode information using legacy fields (`models` and `modes`) rather than the modern `configOptions` array, and it does not support the `session/set_config_option` method for making dynamic changes.

I have already implemented logic to read these legacy fields and have added error handling to fallback to `extMethod` when the primary SDK method fails. Now, I need to rebuild the agent, verify that the UI elements exist, and debug why the selectors are not appearing. I will start by reading the main agent file to check the current implementation of the `newSession` response handler, which is responsible for extracting the model and mode information from the ACP server's response.

<skill_content name="llm-vcs-session-saver">
# Skill: llm-vcs-session-saver

# LLM VCS Session Saver

Saves an LLM conversation session as a flat text file optimized for version control: one line per message, unique timestamped filename, no identifying information. Works with any VCS — Git, SVN, Mercurial, Perforce, and others.

---

## Output File Path

Save the session file to:

```
llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
```

- `YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS` — timestamp of when the session was saved (UTC)
- `XXXX` — 4-character random alphanumeric string to prevent collisions
- Do **not** include usernames, email addresses, or any identifying info in the filename or content

Example: `llm-sessions/2025-04-01-143022-k7qr-session.txt`

---

## Content Format

Each message must be written as a **single line** in this exact format:

```
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] User: <message text>
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LLM: <message text>
```

### Rules

- **Chronological order** — messages appear in the order they occurred
- **Timestamps** — use `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` format (UTC preferred)
- **Speaker labels** — only `User:` and `LLM:` (no other labels or roles)
- **One line per message** — newlines within a message must be replaced with a space or `\n` literal so each message stays on one line
- **No summarizing** — reproduce messages fully and verbatim
- **No extra formatting** — no markdown, no section headers, no blank lines between messages
- **No metadata block** — do not prepend titles, model names, or session IDs

### Example Output

```
[2025-04-01 14:30:01] User: What is the capital of France?
[2025-04-01 14:30:03] LLM: The capital of France is Paris.
[2025-04-01 14:30:10] User: And what's the population?
[2025-04-01 14:30:12] LLM: Paris has a population of approximately 2.1 million in the city proper.
```

---

## Git Readiness

- The file can be written directly and committed with no post-processing
- One-line-per-message ensures Git diffs are clean and human-readable — each changed message shows as exactly one modified line
- The `llm-sessions/` directory should be committed as part of the repo (not gitignored)

---

## Commit Workflow

Every time the user asks to make a Git commit — for any reason, even if unrelated to sessions — run this skill first, then include the session file in the commit.

Steps to follow on every commit:

1. **Generate the session file** using the path format and content rules above
2. **Stage the session file** along with the user's other changes:
   ```bash
   git add llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
   git add <any other files the user wants to commit>
   ```
3. **Commit everything together**:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "<user's commit message>"
   ```

The session file should reflect the full conversation up to and including the commit request itself.

---

## Multi-Session Safety

- Filenames are unique by design (timestamp + random ID), so concurrent sessions will never overwrite each other
- No locking or coordination needed between sessions
- If two sessions start in the same second, the random suffix (`XXXX`) prevents collision

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/.config/opencode/skills/session-history
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

## Agent (2026-04-04 11:29:36)

---
#### Goal

Implement session model and mode selector dropdowns in the MIMO platform that allow users to dynamically switch between available LLM models and operational modes (like "build" vs "plan") during an active session.

#### Instructions

- Use the OpenSpec change located at `openspec/changes/session-model-mode-selector/` for reference on requirements and design decisions
- The implementation must support both modern ACP format (`configOptions`) and legacy format (`models`/`modes` fields) since opencode ACP server uses the legacy format
- Model changes use `session/set_model` with parameter `modelId`
- Mode changes use `session/set_mode` with parameter `modeId` (NOT `mode` - this was a critical fix)
- State is managed in-memory only (no database persistence) via `sessionStateService`
- WebSocket protocol: agent sends `session_initialized` on connect, UI sends `set_model`/`set_mode`, agent forwards to ACP

#### Discoveries

- **Opencode ACP server uses legacy format**: Returns `models` and `modes` fields instead of `configOptions` in `NewSessionResponse`
- **Opencode doesn't support `session/set_config_option`**: Must use `extMethod()` with `session/set_model` and `session/set_mode` endpoints
- **Parameter naming is critical**: Opencode expects `modeId` not `mode` for the mode change endpoint (error was: "Invalid input: expected string, received undefined" for `modeId`)
- **SDK method exists but server doesn't support it**: `setSessionConfigOption` is available in SDK but returns "Method not found" from opencode server
- **Fallback strategy**: Try SDK method first, catch `-32601` error, then retry with `extMethod()`

## Accomplished

✅ **Completed:**
- Created OpenSpec change with proposal, design, specs, and tasks
- Implemented model/mode state extraction in mimo-agent (supports both formats)
- Added WebSocket handlers for `session_initialized`, `set_model`, `set_mode`, `request_state`
- Created `sessionStateService` for shared in-memory state management
- Built UI components (SessionDetailPage) with selectors that show "Not configured" placeholder
- Added JavaScript handlers in chat.js to populate selectors when state arrives
- Implemented graceful fallback from `setSessionConfigOption` to `extMethod` for opencode compatibility
- Fixed parameter name from `mode` to `modeId` for mode changes
- **Both model AND mode switching now work correctly with opencode**

📋 **Status:** Feature is complete and fully functional

#### Relevant files / directories

**Core Implementation:**
- `packages/mimo-agent/src/index.ts` - Agent logic, ACP communication, model/mode change handlers
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/sessions/state.ts` - In-memory state service (NEW FILE)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/index.tsx` - WebSocket message handlers for agent communication
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/sessions/routes.tsx` - Passes state to SessionDetailPage

**UI Components:**
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/components/SessionDetailPage.tsx` - Selector dropdowns in session header
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/components/Layout.tsx` - CSS styles for selectors

**Frontend JavaScript:**
- `packages/mimo-platform/public/js/chat.js` - WebSocket client, selector population, event handlers

**Documentation:**
- `openspec/changes/session-model-mode-selector/` - Complete OpenSpec change with:
  - `proposal.md` - Why and what
  - `design.md` - Architecture and decisions
  - `specs/session-model-selector/spec.md` - Requirements
  - `specs/session-mode-selector/spec.md` - Requirements
  - `tasks.md` - Implementation checklist (all complete)

**Commits Made:**
- `feat(session): add model and mode selector dropdowns`
- `feat(agent): add support for legacy ACP models/modes format`
- `fix(agent): correct parameter name for mode change in opencode`

45: class MimoAgent {
46:   private ws: WebSocket | null = null;
47:   private config: AgentConfig;
48:   private sessions: Map<string, SessionInfo> = new Map();
49:   private pendingChanges: FileChange[] = [];
50:   private changeTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;

55:   constructor() {
56:     this.config = this.parseArgs();

59:   private parseArgs(): AgentConfig {
60:     const args = process.argv.slice(2);
61:     const config: Partial<AgentConfig> = {};
62: 
63:     for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
64:       const arg = args[i];
65:       if (arg === "--token" && i + 1 < args.length) {
66:         config.token = args[++i];
67:       } else if (arg === "--platform" && i + 1 < args.length) {
68:         config.platform = args[++i];
69:       } else if (arg === "--workdir" && i + 1 < args.length) {
70:         config.workDir = args[++i];
71:       }
72:     }
73: 
74:     if (!config.token) {
75:       throw new Error("Missing required argument: --token");
76:     }
77:     if (!config.platform) {
78:       throw new Error("Missing required argument: --platform");
79:     }
80: 
81:     // Default workDir to current directory if not specified
82:     if (!config.workDir) {
83:       config.workDir = process.cwd();
84:     }
85: 
86:     return config as AgentConfig;
87:   }
88: 
89:   async start(): Promise<void> {
90:     console.log("[mimo-agent] Starting...");
91:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Platform: ${this.config.platform}`);
92:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] WorkDir: ${this.config.workDir}`);
93: 
94:     // Connect to platform
95:     await this.connect();
96: 
97:     // Handle graceful shutdown
98:     this.setupShutdownHandlers();
99:   }
100: 
101:   private async connect(): Promise<void> {
102:     const wsUrl = `${this.config.platform}?token=${this.config.token}`;
103:     
104:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
105:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Connecting to ${this.config.platform}...`);
106:       
107:       this.ws = new WebSocket(wsUrl);
108: 
109:       this.ws.on("open", () => {
110:         console.log("[mimo-agent] Connected to platform");
111:         this.reconnectAttempts = 0;
112:         
113:         // Send agent_ready message with workdir
114:         this.send({
115:           type: "agent_ready",
116:           workdir: this.config.workDir,
117:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
118:         });
119:         
120:         resolve();
121:       });
122: 
123:       this.ws.on("message", (data: Buffer) => {
124:         try {
125:           const message = JSON.parse(data.toString());
126:           this.handleMessage(message);
127:         } catch (error) {
128:           console.error("[mimo-agent] Failed to parse message:", error);
129:         }
130:       });
131: 
132:       this.ws.on("close", (code: number, reason: Buffer) => {
133:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] Disconnected (code: ${code})`);
134:         this.handleDisconnect();
135:       });
136: 
137:       this.ws.on("error", (error: Error) => {
138:         console.error("[mimo-agent] WebSocket error:", error.message);
139:         reject(error);
140:       });
141: 
142:       // Connection timeout
143:       setTimeout(() => {
144:         if (this.ws?.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) {
145:           reject(new Error("Connection timeout"));
146:         }
147:       }, 10000);
148:     });
149:   }
150: 
151:   private handleMessage(message: any): void {
152:     switch (message.type) {
153:       case "ping":
154:         this.send({ type: "pong" });
155:         break;
156: 
157:       case "session_ready":
158:         this.handleSessionReady(message);
159:         break;
160: 
161:       case "acp_request":
162:         this.handleAcpRequest(message);
163:         break;
164: 
165:       case "cancel_request":
166:         this.handleCancelRequest(message);
167:         break;
168: 
169:       case "file_sync_request":
170:         this.handleFileSyncRequest(message);
171:         break;
172: 
173:       case "user_message":
174:         this.handleUserMessage(message);
175:         break;
176: 
177:       case "set_model":
178:         this.handleSetModel(message);
179:         break;
180: 
181:       case "set_mode":
182:         this.handleSetMode(message);
183:         break;
184: 
185:       case "request_state":
186:         this.handleRequestState(message);
187:         break;
188: 
189:       default:
190:         console.log("[mimo-agent] Unknown message type:", message.type);
191:     }
192:   }
193: 
194:   private async handleSessionReady(message: any): Promise<void> {
195:     const { platformUrl, sessions } = message;
196:     
197:     if (!sessions || sessions.length === 0) {
198:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessions assigned");
199:       return;
200:     }
201: 
202:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Received ${sessions.length} session(s)`);
203: 
204:     const sessionIds: string[] = [];
205: 
206:     for (const session of sessions) {
207:       const { sessionId, port } = session;
208:       
209:       try {
210:         // Checkout path is {workdir}/{sessionId}
211:         const checkoutPath = join(this.config.workDir, sessionId);
212:         // Parse platformUrl to get just the host, then add port
213:         const platformHost = platformUrl.replace(/^https?:\/\//, '').replace(/\/+$/, '');
214:         const fossilUrl = `http://${platformHost.split(':')[0]}:${port}/`;
215:         
216:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] Setting up session ${sessionId}`);
217:         console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Fossil URL: ${fossilUrl}`);
218:         console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Checkout: ${checkoutPath}`);
219: 
220:         // Clone from fossil server
221:         const repoPath = join(checkoutPath, "..", `${sessionId}.fossil`);
222:         
223:         if (existsSync(repoPath)) {
224:           console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Fossil repo already exists, opening checkout`);
225:           // Create checkout directory if needed
226:           if (!existsSync(checkoutPath)) {
227:             mkdirSync(checkoutPath, { recursive: true });
228:           }
229:           
230:           // Open existing repo
231:           try {
232:             execSync(`fossil open ${repoPath}`, {
233:               cwd: checkoutPath,
234:               stdio: "pipe",
235:             });
236:           } catch {
237:             // Already open or other error, continue
238:           }
239:         } else if (existsSync(join(checkoutPath, ".fossil"))) {
240:           console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Checkout already exists, ensuring open`);
241:           // Checkout already exists, make sure it's open
242:           try {
243:             execSync(`fossil open`, {
244:               cwd: checkoutPath,
245:               stdio: "pipe",
246:             });
247:           } catch {
248:             // Already open or other error, continue
249:           }
250:         } else {
251:           console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Cloning from fossil`);
252:           // Clone from fossil server
253:           execSync(`fossil clone ${fossilUrl} ${repoPath}`, {
254:             stdio: "pipe",
255:           });
256:           
257:           // Create checkout directory and open
258:           if (!existsSync(checkoutPath)) {
259:             mkdirSync(checkoutPath, { recursive: true });
260:           }
261:           
262:           execSync(`fossil open ${repoPath}`, {
263:             cwd: checkoutPath,
264:             stdio: "pipe",
265:           });
266:         }
267: 
268:         // Store session info (will update acpProcess after spawn)
269:         const sessionInfo: SessionInfo = {
270:           sessionId,
271:           checkoutPath,
272:           fossilUrl,
273:           acpProcess: null,
274:           fileWatcher: null,
275:         };
276:         this.sessions.set(sessionId, sessionInfo);
277: 
278:         // Spawn ACP process in checkout directory
279:         console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Spawning ACP process (opencode acp)`);
280:         this.spawnAcpProcess(sessionInfo);
281: 
282:         // Start file watcher for this session
283:         this.startFileWatcher(sessionId, checkoutPath);
284: 
285:         sessionIds.push(sessionId);
286:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] Session ${sessionId} ready`);
287:       } catch (error) {
288:         console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to setup session ${sessionId}:`, error);
289:         this.send({
290:           type: "session_error",
291:           sessionId,
292:           error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
293:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
294:         });
295:       }
296:     }
297: 
298:     // Send confirmation to platform
299:     this.send({
300:       type: "agent_sessions_ready",
301:       sessionIds,
302:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
303:     });
304:   }
305: 
306:   private startFileWatcher(sessionId: string, checkoutPath: string): void {
307:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Starting file watcher for session ${sessionId}`);
308: 
309:     const watcher = watch(
310:       checkoutPath,
311:       { recursive: true },
312:       (eventType: string, filename: string | null) => {
313:         if (!filename) return;
314:         
315:         // Skip hidden files and common ignore patterns
316:         if (filename.startsWith(".") || filename.includes("/.") || 
317:             filename.includes("node_modules") || filename.includes("__pycache__") ||
318:             filename.endsWith(".tmp") || filename.endsWith("~")) {
319:           return;
320:         }
321: 
322:         const change: FileChange = {
323:           path: filename,
324:           isNew: eventType === "rename",
325:           deleted: false,
326:         };
327: 
328:         this.pendingChanges.push(change);
329:         
330:         if (this.changeTimeout) {
331:           clearTimeout(this.changeTimeout);
332:         }
333:         
334:         this.changeTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
335:           this.flushPendingChanges(sessionId);
336:         }, 500);
337:       }
338:     );
339: 
340:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
341:     if (session) {
342:       session.fileWatcher = watcher;
343:     }
344:   }
345: 
346:   private flushPendingChanges(sessionId: string): void {
347:     if (this.pendingChanges.length === 0) return;
348: 
349:     const changes = [...this.pendingChanges];
350:     this.pendingChanges = [];
351: 
352:     // Deduplicate changes
353:     const uniqueChanges = new Map<string, FileChange>();
354:     for (const change of changes) {
355:       uniqueChanges.set(change.path, change);
356:     }
357: 
358:     this.send({
359:       type: "file_changed",
360:       sessionId,
361:       files: Array.from(uniqueChanges.values()),
362:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
363:     });
364:   }
365: 
366:   private spawnAcpProcess(session: SessionInfo): void {
367:     if (session.acpProcess) {
368:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Terminating existing ACP process for ${session.sessionId}`);
369:       session.acpProcess.kill();
370:       session.acpProcess = null;
371:     }
372: 
373:     const acpCommand = "opencode";
374:     const acpArgs = ["acp"];
375: 
376:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Spawning ACP: ${acpCommand} ${acpArgs.join(" ")}`);
377:     console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Working directory: ${session.checkoutPath}`);
378: 
379:     const process = spawn(acpCommand, acpArgs, {
380:       cwd: session.checkoutPath,
381:       stdio: ["pipe", "pipe", "pipe"],
382:     });
383:     session.acpProcess = process;
384: 
385:     // Create ACP stream for stdio
386:     const input = Writable.toWeb(process.stdin!);
387:     const output = Readable.toWeb(process.stdout!);
388:     const stream = acp.ndJsonStream(input, output);
389: 
390:     // Create client-side connection
391:     const client: acp.Client = {
392:       requestPermission: async (params) => {
393:         // Auto-approve for now - you might want to route this to the platform
394:         return {
395:           outcome: { outcome: "approved", optionId: "allow" },
396:         };
397:       },
398:       sessionUpdate: async (params) => {
399:         const update = params.update as any;
400:         const updateType = update?.sessionUpdate;
401: 
402:         // Skip available_commands_update for now
403:         if (updateType === "available_commands_update") {
404:           return;
405:         }
406: 
407:         // Parse and route different update types
408:         if (updateType === "agent_thought_chunk") {
409:           // Group thoughts - send start on first chunk
410:           if (!session.currentThoughtBuffer) {
411:             session.currentThoughtBuffer = "";
412:             this.send({
413:               type: "thought_start",
414:               sessionId: session.sessionId,
415:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
416:             });
417:           }
418:           session.currentThoughtBuffer += update.content?.text || "";
419:           this.send({
420:             type: "thought_chunk",
421:             sessionId: session.sessionId,
422:             content: update.content?.text || "",
423:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
424:           });
425:           return;
426:         }
427: 
428:         if (updateType === "agent_message_chunk") {
429:           // If we were buffering thoughts, end them first
430:           if (session.currentThoughtBuffer) {
431:             this.send({
432:               type: "thought_end",
433:               sessionId: session.sessionId,
434:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
435:             });
436:             session.currentThoughtBuffer = "";
437:           }
438:           this.send({
439:             type: "message_chunk",
440:             sessionId: session.sessionId,
441:             content: update.content?.text || "",
442:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
443:           });
444:           return;
445:         }
446: 
447:         if (updateType === "usage_update") {
448:           this.send({
449:             type: "usage_update",
450:             sessionId: session.sessionId,
451:             usage: {
452:               cost: update.cost || {},
453:               size: update.size,
454:               used: update.used,
455:             },
456:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
457:           });
458:           return;
459:         }
460: 
461:         // Fallback for other update types - send minimal info
462:         this.send({
463:           type: "acp_response",
464:           sessionId: session.sessionId,
465:           content: updateType || "update",
466:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
467:         });
468:       },
469:     };
470: 
471:     session.acpConnection = new acp.ClientSideConnection(() => client, stream);
472: 
473:     // Initialize the ACP connection
474:     session.acpConnection
475:       .initialize({
476:         protocolVersion: acp.PROTOCOL_VERSION,
477:         clientInfo: { name: "mimo-agent", version: "0.1.0" },
478:       })
479:       .then((response) => {
480:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP initialized for ${session.sessionId}:`, response.protocolVersion);
481:         
482:         // Create session - mcpServers is required by schema
483:         return session.acpConnection!.newSession({
484:           cwd: session.checkoutPath,
485:           mcpServers: [],
486:         });
487:       })
488:       .then((sessionResponse) => {
489:         session.acpSessionId = sessionResponse.sessionId;
490:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP session created: ${sessionResponse.sessionId}`);
491:         
492:         // DEBUG: Log full response
493:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] sessionResponse keys:`, Object.keys(sessionResponse));
494:         
495:         // Extract model and mode state from configOptions (modern) or legacy fields
496:         if (sessionResponse.configOptions) {
497:           console.log(`[mimo-agent] Using modern configOptions format`);
498:           const modelConfig = sessionResponse.configOptions.find(
499:             (opt) => opt.category === "model" && opt.type === "select"
500:           );
501:           const modeConfig = sessionResponse.configOptions.find(
502:             (opt) => opt.category === "mode" && opt.type === "select"
503:           );
504:           
505:           if (modelConfig && modelConfig.type === "select") {
506:             const availableOptions = Array.isArray(modelConfig.options)
507:               ? modelConfig.options.map((opt: any) => ({
508:                   value: opt.value,
509:                   name: opt.name,
510:                   description: opt.description,
511:                 }))
512:               : [];
513:             
514:             const currentModelId = modelConfig.currentValue || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
515:             
516:             session.modelState = {
517:               currentModelId,
518:               availableModels: availableOptions,
519:               optionId: modelConfig.id,
520:             };
521:             
522:             console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model state: ${currentModelId} (${availableOptions.length} available)`);
523:           }
524:           
525:           if (modeConfig && modeConfig.type === "select") {
526:             const availableOptions = Array.isArray(modeConfig.options)
527:               ? modeConfig.options.map((opt: any) => ({
528:                   value: opt.value,
529:                   name: opt.name,
530:                   description: opt.description,
531:                 }))
532:               : [];
533:             
534:             const currentModeId = modeConfig.currentValue || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
535:             
536:             session.modeState = {
537:               currentModeId,
538:               availableModes: availableOptions,
539:               optionId: modeConfig.id,
540:             };
541:             
542:             console.log(`[mimo-agent] Mode state: ${currentModeId} (${availableOptions.length} available)`);
543:           }
544:         } 
545:         // Fallback to legacy fields (models/modes)
546:         else if (sessionResponse.models) {
547:           console.log(`[mimo-agent] Using legacy models/modes format`);
548:           
549:           // Extract model state from legacy 'models' field
550:           if (sessionResponse.models) {
551:             const models = sessionResponse.models;
552:             const availableOptions = Array.isArray(models.availableModels)
553:               ? models.availableModels.map((m: any) => ({
554:                   value: m.modelId || m.id,
555:                   name: m.name || m.modelId || m.id,
556:                   description: m.description,
557:                 }))
558:               : [];
559:             
560:             const currentModelId = models.currentModelId || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
561:             
562:             session.modelState = {
563:               currentModelId,
564:               availableModels: availableOptions,
565:               optionId: "model",  // Legacy uses hardcoded ID
566:             };
567:             
568:             console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model state (legacy): ${currentModelId} (${availableOptions.length} available)`);
569:           }
570:           
571:           // Extract mode state from legacy 'modes' field
572:           if (sessionResponse.modes) {
573:             const modes = sessionResponse.modes;
574:             const availableOptions = Array.isArray(modes.availableModes)
575:               ? modes.availableModes.map((m: any) => ({
576:                   value: m.id,
577:                   name: m.name || m.id,
578:                   description: m.description,
579:                 }))
580:               : [];
581:             
582:             const currentModeId = modes.currentModeId || (availableOptions[0]?.value ?? "");
583:             
584:             session.modeState = {
585:               currentModeId,
586:               availableModes: availableOptions,
587:               optionId: "mode",  // Legacy uses hardcoded ID
588:             };
589:             
590:             console.log(`[mimo-agent] Mode state (legacy): ${currentModeId} (${availableOptions.length} available)`);
591:           }
592:         } else {
593:           console.log(`[mimo-agent] No configOptions or legacy fields found`);
594:         }
595:         
596:         // Always send session initialized message (even if no configOptions)
597:         this.send({
598:           type: "session_initialized",
599:           sessionId: session.sessionId,
600:           modelState: session.modelState,
601:           modeState: session.modeState,
602:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
603:         });
604:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] Sent session_initialized for ${session.sessionId}`);
605:       })
606:       .catch((err) => {
607:         console.error(`[mimo-agent] ACP init error for ${session.sessionId}:`, err);
608:       });
609: 
610:     process.stderr?.on("data", (data: Buffer) => {
611:       console.error(`[mimo-agent] ACP stderr (${session.sessionId}):`, data.toString());
612:     });
613: 
614:     process.on("close", (code: number | null) => {
615:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP process exited for ${session.sessionId} with code ${code}`);
616:       session.acpProcess = null;
617:       session.acpConnection = undefined;
618:       session.acpSessionId = undefined;
619:     });
620: 
621:     process.on("error", (err: Error) => {
622:       console.error(`[mimo-agent] ACP process error for ${session.sessionId}:`, err.message);
623:       this.send({
624:         type: "session_error",
625:         sessionId: session.sessionId,
626:         error: `ACP process error: ${err.message}`,
627:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
628:       });
629:     });
630:   }
631: 
632:   private handleAcpRequest(message: any): void {
633:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
634:     if (!sessionId) {
635:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessionId in acp_request");
636:       return;
637:     }
638: 
639:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
640:     if (!session) {
641:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId}`);
642:       return;
643:     }
644: 
645:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Received acp_request for session ${sessionId}`);
646:     this.spawnAcpProcess(session);
647:   }
648: 
649:   private handleCancelRequest(message: any): void {
650:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
651:     if (!sessionId) {
652:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessionId in cancel_request");
653:       return;
654:     }
655: 
656:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
657:     if (!session) {
658:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId}`);
659:       return;
660:     }
661: 
662:     if (session.acpProcess) {
663:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Cancelling ACP request for ${sessionId}...`);
664:       session.acpProcess.kill("SIGTERM");
665:       session.acpProcess = null;
666:       
667:       this.send({
668:         type: "acp_cancelled",
669:         sessionId,
670:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
671:       });
672:     } else {
673:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No active ACP request for ${sessionId}`);
674:     }
675:   }
676: 
677:   private handleFileSyncRequest(message: any): void {
678:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
679:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] File sync request received for session ${sessionId || "unknown"}`);
680:   }
681: 
682:   private handleUserMessage(message: any): void {
683:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
684:     const content = message.content;
685: 
686:     if (!sessionId) {
687:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessionId in user_message");
688:       return;
689:     }
690: 
691:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
692:     if (!session) {
693:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId}`);
694:       this.send({
695:         type: "error_response",
696:         sessionId,
697:         error: `Unknown session: ${sessionId}`,
698:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
699:       });
700:       return;
701:     }
702: 
703:     // Send prompt via ACP connection
704:     if (session.acpConnection && session.acpSessionId) {
705:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Sending prompt to ACP for session ${sessionId}`);
706:       session.acpConnection
707:         .prompt({
708:           sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
709:           prompt: [
710:             { type: "text", text: content },
711:           ],
712:         })
713:         .then((response) => {
714:           console.log(`[mimo-agent] Prompt completed: ${response.stopReason}`);
715:         })
716:         .catch((err) => {
717:           console.error(`[mimo-agent] Prompt error:`, err);
718:           this.send({
719:             type: "error_response",
720:             sessionId,
721:             error: `ACP prompt error: ${err.message}`,
722:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
723:           });
724:         });
725:     } else {
726:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No ACP connection for session ${sessionId}`);
727:     }
728:   }
729: 
730:   private async handleSetModel(message: any): Promise<void> {
731:     const { sessionId, modelId } = message;
732:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Set model request for session ${sessionId}: ${modelId}`);
733: 
734:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
735:     if (!session) {
736:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId}`);
737:       return;
738:     }
739: 
740:     if (!session.acpConnection || !session.acpSessionId) {
741:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No ACP connection for session ${sessionId}`);
742:       return;
743:     }
744: 
745:     if (!session.modelState) {
746:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No model state for session ${sessionId}`);
747:       return;
748:     }
749: 
750:     try {
751:       // Try using the SDK method first
752:       if (session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption) {
753:         try {
754:           await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
755:             sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
756:             configId: session.modelState.optionId,
757:             value: modelId,
758:           });
759:         } catch (err: any) {
760:           // If method not found, fall back to extMethod
761:           if (err.code === -32601 || err.message?.includes("Method not found")) {
762:             console.log(`[mimo-agent] setSessionConfigOption not supported, trying extMethod session/set_model`);
763:             await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_model", {
764:               sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
765:               modelId: modelId,
766:             });
767:           } else {
768:             throw err;
769:           }
770:         }
771:       } else {
772:         // Fallback: use extMethod for legacy session/set_model
773:         await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_model", {
774:           sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
775:           modelId: modelId,
776:         });
777:       }
778: 
779:       // Update local state
780:       session.modelState.currentModelId = modelId;
781: 
782:       // Notify platform of the change
783:       this.send({
784:         type: "model_state",
785:         sessionId,
786:         modelState: session.modelState,
787:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
788:       });
789: 
790:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Model changed to ${modelId}`);
791:     } catch (err: any) {
792:       console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set model:`, err);
793:       this.send({
794:         type: "error_response",
795:         sessionId,
796:         error: `Failed to set model: ${err.message}`,
797:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
798:       });
799:     }
800:   }
801: 
802:   private async handleSetMode(message: any): Promise<void> {
803:     const { sessionId, modeId } = message;
804:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Set mode request for session ${sessionId}: ${modeId}`);
805: 
806:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
807:     if (!session) {
808:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId}`);
809:       return;
810:     }
811: 
812:     if (!session.acpConnection || !session.acpSessionId) {
813:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No ACP connection for session ${sessionId}`);
814:       return;
815:     }
816: 
817:     if (!session.modeState) {
818:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No mode state for session ${sessionId}`);
819:       return;
820:     }
821: 
822:     try {
823:       // Try using the SDK method first
824:       if (session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption) {
825:         try {
826:           await session.acpConnection.setSessionConfigOption({
827:             sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
828:             configId: session.modeState.optionId,
829:             value: modeId,
830:           });
831:         } catch (err: any) {
832:           // If method not found, fall back to extMethod
833:           if (err.code === -32601 || err.message?.includes("Method not found")) {
834:             console.log(`[mimo-agent] setSessionConfigOption not supported, trying extMethod session/set_mode`);
835:             await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_mode", {
836:               sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
837:               modeId: modeId,  // ← was "mode", should be "modeId"
838:             });
839:           } else {
840:             throw err;
841:           }
842:         }
843:       } else {
844:         // Fallback: use extMethod for legacy session/set_mode
845:         await session.acpConnection.extMethod("session/set_mode", {
846:           sessionId: session.acpSessionId,
847:           modeId: modeId,  // ← was "mode", should be "modeId"
848:         });
849:       }
850: 
851:       // Update local state
852:       session.modeState.currentModeId = modeId;
853: 
854:       // Notify platform of the change
855:       this.send({
856:         type: "mode_state",
857:         sessionId,
858:         modeState: session.modeState,
859:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
860:       });
861: 
862:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Mode changed to ${modeId}`);
863:     } catch (err: any) {
864:       console.error(`[mimo-agent] Failed to set mode:`, err);
865:       this.send({
866:         type: "error_response",
867:         sessionId,
868:         error: `Failed to set mode: ${err.message}`,
869:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
870:       });
871:     }
872:   }
873: 
874:   private handleRequestState(message: any): void {
875:     const { sessionId } = message;
876:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
877:     
878:     if (!session) {
879:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId} in request_state`);
880:       return;
881:     }
882:     
883:     // Send current state to platform
884:     this.send({
885:       type: "session_initialized",
886:       sessionId,
887:       modelState: session.modelState,
888:       modeState: session.modeState,
889:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
890:     });
891:     
892:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Sent state for session ${sessionId}`);
893:   }
894: 
895:   private handleDisconnect(): void {
896:     if (this.reconnectAttempts < this.maxReconnectAttempts) {
897:       this.reconnectAttempts++;
898:       const delay = this.reconnectDelay * Math.pow(2, this.reconnectAttempts - 1);
899:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Reconnecting in ${delay}ms (attempt ${this.reconnectAttempts})...`);
900:       
901:       setTimeout(() => {
902:         this.connect().catch((error) => {
903:           console.error("[mimo-agent] Reconnect failed:", error.message);
904:         });
905:       }, delay);
906:     } else {
907:       console.error("[mimo-agent] Max reconnect attempts reached, exiting");
908:       process.exit(1);
909:     }
910:   }
911: 
912:   private send(message: any): void {
913:     if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
914:       this.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message));
915:     } else {
916:       console.log("[mimo-agent] WebSocket not connected, message buffered");
917:     }
918:   }
919: 
920:   private setupShutdownHandlers(): void {
921:     const shutdown = () => {
922:       console.log("[mimo-agent] Shutting down gracefully...");
923:       
924:       // Kill all ACP processes
925:       for (const [sessionId, session] of this.sessions) {
926:         if (session.acpProcess) {
927:           console.log(`[mimo-agent] Killing ACP process for ${sessionId}`);
928:           session.acpProcess.kill("SIGTERM");
929:         }
930:         if (session.fileWatcher) {
931:           session.fileWatcher.close();
932:         }
933:       }
934: 
935:       // Close WebSocket
936:       if (this.ws) {
937:         this.ws.close();
938:       }
939: 
940:       process.exit(0);
941:     };
942: 
943:     process.on("SIGTERM", shutdown);
944:     process.on("SIGINT", shutdown);
945:     process.on("SIGUSR2", shutdown);
946: 
947:     // Handle uncaught errors

import { ModelState, ModeState } from "../types";

  category: string;

    value: string;
    name: string;

  }>;
